from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta
from pathlib import Path

import duckdb
import pytest

from app.storage.facts import EventLog, open_facts


def test_init_schema_creates_tables(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")

    rows = store._conn.execute(
        """
        SELECT table_name
        FROM information_schema.tables
        WHERE table_name IN ('facts', 'events', 'active_fact_keys')
        ORDER BY table_name
        """
    ).fetchall()

    assert rows == [("active_fact_keys",), ("events",), ("facts",)]


def test_upsert_fact_appends_row_and_active_projection(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")

    fact_id = store.upsert_fact(
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="contacts",
        key="manager",
        value={"name": "Hausverwaltung Berlin"},
        source_id="stamm-1",
        source_kind="stammdaten",
        source_locator="/verwaltung/name",
        confidence=0.98,
    )

    fact = store.fetch_fact(fact_id)
    assert fact is not None
    assert fact["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert fact["section"] == "contacts"
    assert fact["key"] == "manager"
    assert fact["value"] == {"name": "Hausverwaltung Berlin"}
    assert fact["source_kind"] == "stammdaten"
    assert fact["confidence"] == 0.98

    active_key = store.fetch_active_key("LIE-001", "contacts", "manager")
    assert active_key is not None
    assert active_key["fact_id"] == fact_id


def test_upsert_fact_with_idempotency_key_is_noop_on_duplicate(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")

    first = store.upsert_fact(
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="finance",
        key="iban",
        value={"iban": "DE02120300000000202051"},
        idempotency_key="env-1:iban",
    )
    second = store.upsert_fact(
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="finance",
        key="iban",
        value={"iban": "DE02120300000000202051"},
        idempotency_key="env-1:iban",
    )

    assert second == first
    assert len(store.list_facts()) == 1
    assert [event["kind"] for event in store.list_events()] == ["fact.asserted"]


def test_duplicate_active_upsert_without_idempotency_is_noop(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")

    first = store.upsert_fact(
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="contacts",
        key="manager",
        value={"name": "Hausverwaltung Berlin"},
        source_id="stamm-1",
        source_kind="stammdaten",
        source_locator="/verwaltung/name",
        confidence=0.98,
    )
    second = store.upsert_fact(
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="contacts",
        key="manager",
        value={"name": "Hausverwaltung Berlin"},
        source_id="stamm-1",
        source_kind="stammdaten",
        source_locator="/verwaltung/name",
        confidence=0.98,
    )

    assert second == first
    assert len(store.list_facts()) == 1
    assert [event["kind"] for event in store.list_events()] == ["fact.asserted"]


def test_upsert_supersedes_active_fact_without_mutating_old_row(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")
    first_at = datetime(2026, 5, 4, 8, 0, tzinfo=UTC)
    second_at = datetime(2026, 5, 4, 9, 0, tzinfo=UTC)

    old_fact_id = store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id="fact-old",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="contacts",
        key="manager",
        value={"name": "Old Verwaltung"},
        source_id="mail-1",
        source_kind="email",
        source_locator="message:1",
        asserted_at=first_at,
        idempotency_key="mail-1:manager",
    )
    old_before = store.fetch_fact(old_fact_id)

    new_fact_id = store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id="fact-new",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="contacts",
        key="manager",
        value={"name": "New Verwaltung"},
        source_id="mail-2",
        source_kind="email",
        source_locator="message:2",
        asserted_at=second_at,
        idempotency_key="mail-2:manager",
    )

    assert new_fact_id != old_fact_id
    assert store.fetch_fact(old_fact_id) == old_before
    assert store.fetch_fact(new_fact_id)["supersedes_fact_id"] == old_fact_id
    assert store.fetch_active_key("LIE-001", "contacts", "manager")["fact_id"] == new_fact_id
    assert [fact["fact_id"] for fact in store.list_active_facts("LIE-001")] == [new_fact_id]
    assert [event["kind"] for event in store.list_events()].count("fact.superseded") == 1


def test_active_and_by_section_queries_return_current_projection(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")

    store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id="fact-manager",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="contacts",
        key="manager",
        value={"name": "Hausverwaltung Berlin"},
    )
    store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id="fact-caretaker",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="contacts",
        key="caretaker",
        value={"name": "Facility Team"},
    )
    store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id="fact-iban",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="finance",
        key="iban",
        value={"iban": "DE02120300000000202051"},
    )

    assert [fact["key"] for fact in store.list_active_facts("LIE-001")] == [
        "caretaker",
        "manager",
        "iban",
    ]
    assert [fact["key"] for fact in store.list_active_facts_by_section("LIE-001", "contacts")] == [
        "caretaker",
        "manager",
    ]
    assert [fact["key"] for fact in store.list_facts_by_section("LIE-001", "contacts")] == [
        "caretaker",
        "manager",
    ]


def test_transaction_time_as_of_returns_frozen_snapshot(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")
    first_at = datetime(2026, 5, 4, 8, 0, tzinfo=UTC)
    second_at = datetime(2026, 5, 4, 9, 0, tzinfo=UTC)

    store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id="fact-old",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="finance",
        key="reserve",
        value={"amount_eur": 1000},
        asserted_at=first_at,
    )
    store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id="fact-new",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="finance",
        key="reserve",
        value={"amount_eur": 2000},
        asserted_at=second_at,
    )

    before_supersede = store.list_active_facts_as_of(
        "LIE-001",
        first_at + timedelta(minutes=30),
    )
    after_supersede = store.list_active_facts_as_of(
        "LIE-001",
        second_at + timedelta(minutes=1),
    )

    assert [fact["fact_id"] for fact in before_supersede] == ["fact-old"]
    assert before_supersede[0]["value"] == {"amount_eur": 1000}
    assert [fact["fact_id"] for fact in after_supersede] == ["fact-new"]
    assert after_supersede[0]["value"] == {"amount_eur": 2000}


def test_valid_time_as_of_fails_clearly(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")

    with pytest.raises(NotImplementedError, match="valid-time as-of queries are not supported"):
        store.list_active_facts_as_of(
            "LIE-001",
            datetime(2026, 5, 4, tzinfo=UTC),
            time_axis="valid",
        )


def test_pending_fact_activation_uses_event_and_projection_only(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")

    fact_id = store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id="fact-pending",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="contacts",
        key="manager",
        value={"name": "Needs Review Verwaltung"},
        status="pending",
    )
    before = store.fetch_fact(fact_id)

    store.activate_fact(fact_id, actor="reviewer-1", reason="source verified")

    assert store.fetch_fact(fact_id) == before
    assert store.fetch_active_key("LIE-001", "contacts", "manager")["fact_id"] == fact_id
    assert [fact["current_status"] for fact in store.list_active_facts("LIE-001")] == ["active"]
    assert [event["kind"] for event in store.list_events()] == [
        "fact.asserted",
        "fact.activated",
    ]


def test_active_projection_enforces_one_active_fact_per_key_and_rolls_back(
    tmp_path: Path,
) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")

    store.append_fact(
        fact_id="fact-1",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="finance",
        key="iban",
        value={"iban": "DE02120300000000202051"},
    )

    with pytest.raises(duckdb.ConstraintException):
        store.append_fact(
            fact_id="fact-2",
            property_id="LIE-001",
            section="finance",
            key="iban",
            value={"iban": "DE02120300000000202052"},
        )

    assert [fact["fact_id"] for fact in store.list_facts()] == ["fact-1"]
    assert [event["kind"] for event in store.list_events()] == ["fact.asserted"]


def test_event_replay_rebuilds_active_projection_consistently(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")

    store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id="fact-old",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="finance",
        key="reserve",
        value={"amount_eur": 1000},
    )
    store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id="fact-new",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="finance",
        key="reserve",
        value={"amount_eur": 2000},
    )
    pending_id = store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id="fact-pending",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="contacts",
        key="manager",
        value={"name": "Verified Verwaltung"},
        status="pending",
    )
    store.activate_fact(pending_id)

    replayed = store.replay_active_projection()
    assert replayed == store.list_active_keys()

    store._conn.execute("DELETE FROM active_fact_keys")
    assert store.list_active_keys() == []

    store.rebuild_active_projection()
    assert store.list_active_keys() == replayed


def test_event_log_emit_appends_immutable_events(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")
    event_log = EventLog(store._conn)

    first = event_log.emit("gap_signal", {"property_id": "LIE-001", "missing": "iban"})
    second = event_log.emit("gap_signal", {"property_id": "LIE-001", "missing": "verwalter"})

    events = store.list_events()
    assert first != second
    assert len(events) == 2
    assert [event["kind"] for event in events] == ["gap_signal", "gap_signal"]
    assert events[0]["payload"] == {"property_id": "LIE-001", "missing": "iban"}
    assert events[1]["payload"] == {"property_id": "LIE-001", "missing": "verwalter"}


def test_existing_fact_row_is_not_mutated_on_conflicting_duplicate(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(tmp_path / "facts.duckdb")

    fact_id = store.append_fact(
        fact_id="fact-1",
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section="contacts",
        key="manager",
        value={"name": "Original Verwaltung"},
    )
    before = store.fetch_fact(fact_id)

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="fact_id already exists"):
        store.append_fact(
            fact_id="fact-1",
            property_id="LIE-001",
            section="contacts",
            key="manager",
            value={"name": "Changed Verwaltung"},
        )

    assert store.fetch_fact(fact_id) == before
    assert len(store.list_facts()) == 1

51	            "question": "Which insurance policy is current?",
52	            "source_id": "msg-7",

94	            "section": "zeta",

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import anyio
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict

from app.render import render_building_markdown, write_building_markdown


class MiniCitation(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True)

    source_id: str
    source_kind: str
    source_uri: str | None = None
    locator: str | None = None
    quote: str | None = None


class MiniFact(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True)

    property_id: str
    section: str
    key: str
    value: object | None = None
    status: str = "active"
    citation: MiniCitation
    confidence: float | None = None


async def test_write_building_markdown_renders_active_and_pending_records(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    records = [
        {
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "finance",
            "key": "hausgeld",
            "value": {"amount_eur": 1200, "period": "monthly"},
            "status": "active",
            "source_id": "stamm-1",
            "source_kind": "stammdaten",
            "source_locator": "/finance/hausgeld",
            "confidence": 0.98,
        },
        {
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "contracts",
            "key": "insurance_policy",
            "status": "pending",
            "question": "Which insurance policy is current?",
            "source_id": "msg-7",
            "source_kind": "email",
            "source_locator": "paragraph 3",
            "confidence": 0.51,
        },
        MiniFact(
            property_id="LIE-001",
            section="contacts",
            key="emergency_phone",
            value="030 123456",
            status="pending",
            citation=MiniCitation(
                source_id="msg-8",
                source_kind="email",
                source_uri="memory://payloads/msg-8",
                locator="line 12",
            ),
            confidence=0.42,
        ),
    ]

    path = await write_building_markdown(tmp_path, "LIE-001", records)
    text = await anyio.Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")

    assert path == tmp_path / "LIE-001" / "building.md"
    assert (
        "### Active Facts\n\n- `hausgeld`: {\"amount_eur\": 1200, \"period\": \"monthly\"}"
        in text
    )
    assert (
        "### Pending Questions\n\n- `insurance_policy`: Which insurance policy is current?"
        in text
    )
    assert "### Pending Facts\n\n- `emergency_phone`: 030 123456" in text
    assert "source: stammdaten:stamm-1; locator: `/finance/hausgeld`; confidence: 0.98" in text
    assert "source: [email:msg-8](memory://payloads/msg-8); locator: `line 12`" in text


def test_render_building_markdown_is_stable_for_input_order() -> None:
    records = [
        {
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "zeta",
            "key": "second",
            "value": "B",
            "status": "active",
        },
        {
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "alpha",
            "key": "first",
            "value": "A",
            "status": "active",
        },
    ]

    assert render_building_markdown("LIE-001", records) == render_building_markdown(
        "LIE-001",
        reversed(records),
    )

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated

from anyio.to_thread import run_sync
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from app.core.config import Settings, get_settings
from app.extract.models import Fact, FactStatus, SourceCitation
from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope
from app.schemas.ingest import IngestRequest, IngestResponse
from app.storage.facts import open_facts

router = APIRouter()


@router.post("", response_model=IngestResponse)
async def ingest_envelope(
    request: IngestRequest,
    settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)],
) -> IngestResponse:
    envelope_id = _envelope_id(request.envelope)
    fact = request.fact or _default_fact(request.envelope, envelope_id=envelope_id)

    def write_fact() -> tuple[str, str]:
        store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
        fact_id = store.upsert_fact(
            fact_id=fact.fact_id,
            property_id=fact.property_id,
            section=fact.section,
            key=fact.key,
            value=fact.value,
            status=fact.status.value,
            supersedes_fact_id=fact.supersedes_fact_id,
            asserted_at=fact.asserted_at,
            valid_from=fact.valid_interval.start,
            valid_to=fact.valid_interval.end,
            source_id=fact.citation.source_id,
            source_kind=fact.citation.source_kind,
            source_locator=fact.citation.locator,
            source_excerpt=fact.citation.quote,
            source_timestamp=fact.citation.source_timestamp,
            content_hash=fact.citation.payload_hash,
            extractor="ingest.minimum",
            confidence=fact.confidence,
            idempotency_key=f"fact:{envelope_id}",
        )
        event_id = store.append_event(
            "envelope.ingested",
            request.envelope.model_dump(mode="json"),
            fact_id=fact_id,
            idempotency_key=f"event:{envelope_id}",
        )
        return fact_id, event_id

    fact_id, event_id = await run_sync(write_fact)
    return IngestResponse(envelope_id=envelope_id, fact_id=fact_id, event_id=event_id)


def _envelope_id(envelope: Envelope) -> str:
    return f"env_{envelope.payload_hash[:32].lower()}"


def _default_fact(envelope: Envelope, *, envelope_id: str) -> Fact:
    return Fact(
        property_id=envelope.property,
        section="ingest",
        key=f"{envelope.kind}-{envelope.payload_hash[:12].lower()}",
        value={
            "envelope_id": envelope_id,
            "source": envelope.source,
            "kind": envelope.kind,
            "content_type": envelope.content_type,
            "payload_uri": envelope.payload_uri,
        },
        citation=SourceCitation(
            source_id=envelope.source,
            source_kind=envelope.kind,
            source_uri=envelope.payload_uri,
            locator=envelope.payload_uri or envelope.payload_hash[:16].lower(),
            payload_hash=envelope.payload_hash,
        ),
        confidence=1.0,
        status=FactStatus.ACTIVE,
        asserted_at=envelope.received_at,
    )

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack, asynccontextmanager
from http import HTTPStatus
from pathlib import Path

import structlog
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.responses import RedirectResponse
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from starlette.exceptions import HTTPException as StarletteHTTPException
from starlette.responses import FileResponse, Response
from starlette.types import Lifespan, Scope

from app.api.v1.router import api_router
from app.core.config import REPO_ROOT, get_settings
from app.core.logging import configure_logging
from app.core.middleware import RequestIdMiddleware
from app.mcp import build_mcp

PRM_LOCAL_PATH = "/mcp/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource"

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


class SPAStaticFiles(StaticFiles):
    """Static mount that falls back to index.html for unknown HTML paths (SPA routes)."""

    async def get_response(self, path: str, scope: Scope) -> Response:  # type: ignore[override]
        try:
            return await super().get_response(path, scope)
        except StarletteHTTPException as exc:
            reserved_prefix = path in {"api", "mcp"} or path.startswith(("api/", "mcp/"))
            if (
                exc.status_code != HTTPStatus.NOT_FOUND
                or "." in path.rsplit("/", 1)[-1]
                or reserved_prefix
            ):
                raise
            directory = Path(str(self.directory)) if self.directory else None
            if directory is None:
                raise
            index = directory / "index.html"
            if not index.is_file():
                raise
            return FileResponse(index)


def _make_lifespan(child: Lifespan | None) -> Lifespan:
    @asynccontextmanager
    async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:
        settings = get_settings()
        configure_logging(settings)
        log.info("startup", env=settings.env, version=app.version)
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            if child is not None:
                await stack.enter_async_context(child(app))
            yield
        log.info("shutdown")

    return lifespan


def create_app() -> FastAPI:
    settings = get_settings()

    mcp_app = None
    if settings.mcp_enabled:
        mcp_app = build_mcp(settings).http_app(path="/")

    app = FastAPI(
        title=settings.app_name,
        version=settings.version,
        lifespan=_make_lifespan(mcp_app.lifespan if mcp_app else None),
        docs_url="/docs" if settings.env != "prod" else None,
        redoc_url=None,
    )
    app.add_middleware(RequestIdMiddleware)
    app.include_router(api_router, prefix="/api/v1")
    if mcp_app is not None:

        @app.get("/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource", include_in_schema=False)
        @app.get("/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp", include_in_schema=False)
        async def _prm_redirect() -> RedirectResponse:
            return RedirectResponse(url=PRM_LOCAL_PATH, status_code=307)

        app.mount("/mcp", mcp_app)
    static_dir = REPO_ROOT / "app" / "static"
    if static_dir.is_dir():
        app.mount("/", SPAStaticFiles(directory=static_dir, html=True), name="static")
    return app


app = create_app()

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import cast

from httpx import AsyncClient

from app.storage.facts import open_facts

PAYLOAD_HASH = "b" * 64


def _request_body(payload_hash: str = PAYLOAD_HASH) -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "envelope": {
            "source": "stammdaten",
            "kind": "json",
            "org": "org-001",
            "property": "LIE-001",
            "content_type": "application/json",
            "payload_uri": "data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json",
            "payload_hash": payload_hash,
            "received_at": "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z",
        }
    }


async def test_ingest_writes_one_fact_row(client: AsyncClient, settings) -> None:
    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["envelope_id"] == f"env_{PAYLOAD_HASH[:32]}"

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    facts = store.list_facts()
    events = store.list_events()

    assert len(facts) == 1
    assert facts[0]["fact_id"] == body["fact_id"]
    assert facts[0]["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert facts[0]["section"] == "ingest"
    assert facts[0]["source_kind"] == "json"
    assert len(events) == 1
    assert events[0]["event_id"] == body["event_id"]
    assert events[0]["kind"] == "envelope.ingested"


async def test_ingest_duplicate_payload_is_idempotent(client: AsyncClient, settings) -> None:
    first = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())
    second = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())

    assert first.status_code == 200
    assert second.status_code == 200
    assert second.json() == first.json()

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    assert len(store.list_facts()) == 1
    assert len(store.list_events()) == 1


async def test_ingest_rejects_bad_envelope_id(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    body = _request_body()
    envelope = cast(dict[str, object], body["envelope"])
    envelope["property"] = "../etc/passwd"

    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=body)

    assert response.status_code == 422

from __future__ import annotations

from enum import StrEnum
from typing import Annotated, Literal, Protocol, Self, runtime_checkable

from pydantic import (
    AwareDatetime,
    BaseModel,
    ConfigDict,
    Field,
    JsonValue,
    StringConstraints,
    model_validator,
)

from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope, Identifier, PayloadHash, UriString, utc_now

Payload = str | bytes | JsonValue
ShortText = Annotated[str, StringConstraints(strip_whitespace=True, min_length=1, max_length=512)]
Excerpt = Annotated[str, StringConstraints(strip_whitespace=True, min_length=1, max_length=2000)]
GapSignalKind = Literal["missing", "ambiguous", "conflict", "low_confidence", "unsupported"]


class FactStatus(StrEnum):
    PENDING = "pending"
    ACTIVE = "active"
    REJECTED = "rejected"
    SUPERSEDED = "superseded"


class TimeInterval(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    start: AwareDatetime | None = None
    end: AwareDatetime | None = None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def validate_order(self) -> Self:
        if self.start is not None and self.end is not None and self.end < self.start:
            msg = "interval end must be greater than or equal to start"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        return self


class SourceCitation(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    source_id: Identifier
    source_kind: Identifier
    source_uri: UriString | None = None
    locator: ShortText | None = None
    quote: Excerpt | None = None
    source_timestamp: AwareDatetime | None = None
    payload_hash: PayloadHash | None = None


class TransactionMetadata(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    event_id: Identifier | None = None
    actor: Identifier | None = None
    idempotency_key: Identifier | None = None


class Fact(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    fact_id: Identifier | None = None
    property_id: Identifier
    section: Identifier
    key: Identifier
    value: JsonValue
    citation: SourceCitation
    confidence: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    status: FactStatus = FactStatus.PENDING
    valid_interval: TimeInterval = Field(default_factory=TimeInterval)
    asserted_at: AwareDatetime = Field(default_factory=utc_now)
    transaction: TransactionMetadata = Field(default_factory=TransactionMetadata)
    supersedes_fact_id: Identifier | None = None
    extensions: dict[str, JsonValue] = Field(default_factory=dict)


class GapSignal(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    property_id: Identifier
    section: Identifier
    key: Identifier | None = None
    kind: GapSignalKind
    message: ShortText
    source_id: Identifier | None = None
    confidence: float | None = Field(default=None, ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    extensions: dict[str, JsonValue] = Field(default_factory=dict)


class ExtractionResult(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    facts: list[Fact] = Field(default_factory=list)
    gap_signals: list[GapSignal] = Field(default_factory=list)


@runtime_checkable
class Extractor(Protocol):
    async def extract(self, envelope: Envelope, payload: Payload) -> ExtractionResult:
        """Extract facts and gap signals from one normalized payload."""

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from datetime import UTC, datetime
from typing import Annotated, Self

from pydantic import (
    AfterValidator,
    AwareDatetime,
    BaseModel,
    ConfigDict,
    Field,
    JsonValue,
    StringConstraints,
    field_validator,
    model_validator,
)

ASCII_CONTROL_LIMIT = 32
IDENTIFIER_PATTERN = r"^[A-Za-z0-9](?:[A-Za-z0-9_.-]{0,126}[A-Za-z0-9])?$"


def validate_identifier(value: str) -> str:
    if re.fullmatch(IDENTIFIER_PATTERN, value) is None:
        msg = "identifier must contain only conservative id characters"
        raise ValueError(msg)
    if ".." in value:
        msg = "identifier must not contain traversal segments"
        raise ValueError(msg)
    return value


Identifier = Annotated[
    str,
    StringConstraints(
        strip_whitespace=True,
        min_length=1,
        max_length=128,
        pattern=IDENTIFIER_PATTERN,
    ),
    AfterValidator(validate_identifier),
]
ContentType = Annotated[
    str,
    StringConstraints(
        strip_whitespace=True,
        min_length=3,
        max_length=128,
        pattern=r"^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9!#$&^_.+-]*/[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9!#$&^_.+-]*$",
    ),
]
PayloadHash = Annotated[
    str,
    StringConstraints(
        strip_whitespace=True,
        min_length=64,
        max_length=64,
        pattern=r"^[a-fA-F0-9]{64}$",
    ),
]
UriString = Annotated[str, StringConstraints(strip_whitespace=True, min_length=1, max_length=2048)]


def utc_now() -> datetime:
    return datetime.now(UTC)


class ValidTimeHint(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    start: AwareDatetime | None = None
    end: AwareDatetime | None = None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def validate_order(self) -> Self:
        if self.start is not None and self.end is not None and self.end < self.start:
            msg = "valid time end must be greater than or equal to start"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        return self


class Envelope(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    source: Identifier
    kind: Identifier
    org: Identifier
    property: Identifier
    content_type: ContentType
    payload_uri: UriString | None = None
    payload_hash: PayloadHash
    received_at: AwareDatetime = Field(default_factory=utc_now)
    valid_time_hint: ValidTimeHint | None = None
    extensions: dict[str, JsonValue] = Field(default_factory=dict)

    @field_validator("payload_uri")
    @classmethod
    def reject_control_chars(cls, value: str | None) -> str | None:
        if value is not None and any(ord(char) < ASCII_CONTROL_LIMIT for char in value):
            msg = "payload_uri must not contain control characters"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        return value

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from collections.abc import Iterable, Mapping
4	from dataclasses import dataclass
5	from typing import Literal, Protocol, runtime_checkable
6	
7	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, JsonValue
8	
9	from app.ingest.envelope import Identifier, validate_identifier
10	
11	AdapterType = Literal["extractor", "llm_tool"]
12	
13	
14	@runtime_checkable
15	class Skill(Protocol):
16	    async def __call__(self, input: BaseModel) -> BaseModel:
17	        """Run a deterministic skill against a validated Pydantic input model."""
18	
19	
20	class SkillContract(BaseModel):
21	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)
22	
23	    name: Identifier
24	    adapter_type: AdapterType
25	    input_schema: dict[str, JsonValue] = Field(default_factory=dict)
26	    output_schema: dict[str, JsonValue] = Field(default_factory=dict)
27	
28	
29	@dataclass(frozen=True)
30	class SkillRegistration:
31	    name: str
32	    skill: Skill
33	    adapter_type: AdapterType
34	    input_model: type[BaseModel]
35	    output_model: type[BaseModel]
36	
37	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
38	        object.__setattr__(self, "name", validate_identifier(self.name.strip()))
39	        if not callable(self.skill):
40	            msg = "skill must be callable"
41	            raise TypeError(msg)
42	        if not issubclass(self.input_model, BaseModel):
43	            msg = "input_model must inherit from BaseModel"
44	            raise TypeError(msg)
45	        if not issubclass(self.output_model, BaseModel):
46	            msg = "output_model must inherit from BaseModel"
47	            raise TypeError(msg)
48	
49	    def contract(self) -> SkillContract:
50	        return SkillContract(
51	            name=self.name,
52	            adapter_type=self.adapter_type,
53	            input_schema=self.input_model.model_json_schema(),
54	            output_schema=self.output_model.model_json_schema(),
55	        )
56	
57	
58	class SkillRegistry:
59	    def __init__(self, registrations: Iterable[SkillRegistration] = ()) -> None:
60	        self._registrations: dict[str, SkillRegistration] = {}
61	        for registration in registrations:
62	            self.add(registration)
63	
64	    def add(self, registration: SkillRegistration) -> SkillRegistration:
65	        if registration.name in self._registrations:
66	            msg = f"skill already registered: {registration.name}"
67	            raise ValueError(msg)
68	        self._registrations[registration.name] = registration
69	        return registration
70	
71	    def register(
72	        self,
73	        *,
74	        name: str,
75	        skill: Skill,
76	        adapter_type: AdapterType,
77	        input_model: type[BaseModel],
78	        output_model: type[BaseModel],
79	    ) -> SkillRegistration:
80	        return self.add(
81	            SkillRegistration(
82	                name=name,
83	                skill=skill,
84	                adapter_type=adapter_type,
85	                input_model=input_model,
86	                output_model=output_model,
87	            )
88	        )
89	
90	    def get(self, name: str) -> SkillRegistration:
91	        skill_name = validate_identifier(name.strip())
92	        try:
93	            return self._registrations[skill_name]
94	        except KeyError:
95	            msg = f"unknown skill: {skill_name}"
96	            raise KeyError(msg) from None
97	
98	    def contract(self, name: str) -> SkillContract:
99	        return self.get(name).contract()
100	
101	    def contracts(self) -> list[SkillContract]:
102	        return [registration.contract() for registration in self._registrations.values()]
103	
104	    async def dispatch(self, name: str, input: BaseModel | Mapping[str, object]) -> BaseModel:
105	        registration = self.get(name)
106	        if isinstance(input, BaseModel):
107	            skill_input = registration.input_model.model_validate(input.model_dump())
108	        else:
109	            skill_input = registration.input_model.model_validate(input)
110	        output = await registration.skill(skill_input)
111	        return registration.output_model.model_validate(output)
112

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from collections.abc import Collection, Mapping, Sequence
4	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
5	from typing import Protocol, runtime_checkable
6	
7	from pydantic import BaseModel
8	
9	from app.extract.models import Extractor, Payload
10	from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope, validate_identifier
11	from app.skills.base import SkillRegistry
12	
13	
14	@runtime_checkable
15	class Classifier(Protocol):
16	    async def classify(self, envelope: Envelope, payload: Payload) -> str:
17	        """Classify one envelope before extractor routing."""
18	
19	
20	@runtime_checkable
21	class Observer(Protocol):
22	    async def observe(self, event: str, payload: BaseModel) -> None:
23	        """Observe engine events without owning core control flow."""
24	
25	
26	@dataclass(frozen=True)
27	class VerticalPlugin:
28	    name: str
29	    classifier: Classifier | None = None
30	    extractors: Mapping[str, Extractor] = field(default_factory=dict)
31	    skills: SkillRegistry = field(default_factory=SkillRegistry)
32	    render_template: str = ""
33	    observers: Sequence[Observer] = ()
34	    auth_allowlist: Mapping[str, Collection[str]] = field(default_factory=dict)
35	
36	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
37	        object.__setattr__(self, "name", validate_identifier(self.name.strip()))
38	        object.__setattr__(
39	            self,
40	            "extractors",
41	            {
42	                validate_identifier(name.strip()): extractor
43	                for name, extractor in self.extractors.items()
44	            },
45	        )
46	        object.__setattr__(self, "observers", tuple(self.observers))
47	        object.__setattr__(self, "auth_allowlist", self._normalized_allowlist())
48	
49	    def allows(self, org: str, property_id: str) -> bool:
50	        org_id = validate_identifier(org.strip())
51	        allowed = self.auth_allowlist.get(org_id, frozenset())
52	        return validate_identifier(property_id.strip()) in allowed
53

55	        return {

57	                validate_identifier(property_id.strip()) for property_id in properties
58	            )
59	            for org, properties in self.auth_allowlist.items()
60	        }

from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import UTC, datetime

import pytest
from pydantic import BaseModel, ValidationError

from app.extract.models import (
    ExtractionResult,
    Extractor,
    Fact,
    GapSignal,
    SourceCitation,
    TimeInterval,
)
from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope, ValidTimeHint
from app.plugin import VerticalPlugin
from app.skills.base import SkillRegistry

PAYLOAD_HASH = "a" * 64


def test_envelope_and_fact_validation_accept_contract_shape() -> None:
    envelope = Envelope(
        source="gmail-prod",
        kind="email",
        org="org-001",
        property="LIE-001",
        content_type="message/rfc822",
        payload_uri="memory://payloads/msg-1",
        payload_hash=PAYLOAD_HASH,
        received_at=datetime(2026, 5, 4, tzinfo=UTC),
        valid_time_hint=ValidTimeHint(start=datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)),
        extensions={"thread_id": "abc"},
    )
    fact = Fact(
        property_id=envelope.property,
        section="contracts",
        key="hausgeld",
        value={"amount_eur": 1200, "period": "monthly"},
        citation=SourceCitation(
            source_id=envelope.source,
            source_kind=envelope.kind,
            source_uri=envelope.payload_uri,
            locator="message-id:abc",
            payload_hash=envelope.payload_hash,
        ),
        confidence=0.87,
        valid_interval=TimeInterval(start=datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)),
        asserted_at=datetime(2026, 5, 4, tzinfo=UTC),
    )

    assert envelope.org == "org-001"
    assert fact.value == {"amount_eur": 1200, "period": "monthly"}
    assert fact.citation.payload_hash == PAYLOAD_HASH


def test_bad_id_like_fields_are_rejected() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
        Envelope(
            source="gmail",
            kind="email",
            org="../org",
            property="LIE-001",
            content_type="message/rfc822",
            payload_hash=PAYLOAD_HASH,
        )

    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
        Fact(
            property_id="LIE-001",
            section="contracts",
            key="bad/key",
            value="x",
            citation=SourceCitation(source_id="src-1", source_kind="email"),
            confidence=1.0,
        )

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="identifier"):
        VerticalPlugin(name="bad/name")


async def test_extractor_protocol_returns_facts_and_gap_signals() -> None:
    class StaticExtractor:
        async def extract(
            self,
            envelope: Envelope,
            payload: str | bytes | object,
        ) -> ExtractionResult:
            return ExtractionResult(
                facts=[
                    Fact(
                        property_id=envelope.property,
                        section="contacts",
                        key="verwaltung",
                        value="Example Hausverwaltung",
                        citation=SourceCitation(
                            source_id=envelope.source,
                            source_kind=envelope.kind,
                        ),
                        confidence=0.9,
                    )
                ],
                gap_signals=[
                    GapSignal(
                        property_id=envelope.property,
                        section="contracts",
                        kind="missing",
                        message="No insurance policy found",
                        source_id=envelope.source,
                    )
                ],
            )

    envelope = Envelope(
        source="stammdaten",
        kind="json",
        org="org-001",
        property="LIE-001",
        content_type="application/json",
        payload_hash=PAYLOAD_HASH,
    )
    extractor: Extractor = StaticExtractor()

    result = await extractor.extract(envelope, "{}")

    assert len(result.facts) == 1
    assert result.facts[0].property_id == "LIE-001"
    assert result.gap_signals[0].kind == "missing"


async def test_skill_registry_dispatch_and_schema_contract() -> None:
    class AmountInput(BaseModel):
        amount: int

    class AmountOutput(BaseModel):
        cents: int

    async def to_cents(input: BaseModel) -> BaseModel:
        data = AmountInput.model_validate(input)
        return AmountOutput(cents=data.amount * 100)

    registry = SkillRegistry()
    registry.register(
        name="eur-cents",
        skill=to_cents,
        adapter_type="llm_tool",
        input_model=AmountInput,
        output_model=AmountOutput,
    )

    contract = registry.contract("eur-cents")
    result = await registry.dispatch("eur-cents", {"amount": 12})
    properties = contract.input_schema.get("properties")
    assert isinstance(properties, dict)
    amount_schema = properties.get("amount")
    assert isinstance(amount_schema, dict)

    assert contract.adapter_type == "llm_tool"
    assert amount_schema.get("type") == "integer"
    assert isinstance(result, AmountOutput)
    assert result.cents == 1200


def test_vertical_plugin_keeps_allowlist_and_registries_small() -> None:
    plugin = VerticalPlugin(
        name="property-de",
        extractors={},
        skills=SkillRegistry(),
        render_template="# {{ property_id }}",
        observers=(),
        auth_allowlist={"org-001": {"LIE-001"}},
    )

    assert plugin.allows("org-001", "LIE-001")
    assert not plugin.allows("org-001", "LIE-002")

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import cast

from httpx import AsyncClient

from app.storage.facts import open_facts

PAYLOAD_HASH = "b" * 64


def _request_body(payload_hash: str = PAYLOAD_HASH) -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "envelope": {
            "source": "stammdaten",
            "kind": "json",
            "org": "org-001",
            "property": "LIE-001",
            "content_type": "application/json",
            "payload_uri": "data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json",
            "payload_hash": payload_hash,
            "received_at": "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z",
        }
    }


async def test_ingest_writes_one_fact_row(client: AsyncClient, settings) -> None:

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import cast

from httpx import AsyncClient

from app.storage.facts import open_facts

PAYLOAD_HASH = "b" * 64


def _request_body(payload_hash: str = PAYLOAD_HASH) -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "envelope": {
            "source": "stammdaten",
            "kind": "json",
            "org": "org-001",
            "property": "LIE-001",
            "content_type": "application/json",
            "payload_uri": "data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json",
            "payload_hash": payload_hash,
            "received_at": "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z",
        }
    }


async def test_ingest_writes_one_fact_row(client: AsyncClient, settings) -> None:
    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["envelope_id"] == f"env_{PAYLOAD_HASH[:32]}"

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    facts = store.list_facts()
    events = store.list_events()

    assert len(facts) == 1
    assert facts[0]["fact_id"] == body["fact_id"]
    assert facts[0]["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert facts[0]["section"] == "ingest"
    assert facts[0]["source_kind"] == "json"
    assert [event["kind"] for event in events] == ["fact.asserted", "envelope.ingested"]
    envelope_event = next(event for event in events if event["kind"] == "envelope.ingested")
    assert envelope_event["event_id"] == body["event_id"]
    assert envelope_event["fact_id"] == body["fact_id"]


async def test_ingest_duplicate_payload_is_idempotent(client: AsyncClient, settings) -> None:
    first = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())
    second = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())

    assert first.status_code == 200
    assert second.status_code == 200
    assert second.json() == first.json()

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    assert len(store.list_facts()) == 1
    assert len(store.list_events()) == 1


async def test_ingest_rejects_bad_envelope_id(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    body = _request_body()
    envelope = cast(dict[str, object], body["envelope"])
    envelope["property"] = "../etc/passwd"

    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=body)

    assert response.status_code == 422

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Iterable, Mapping

from app.storage.facts import FactsStore


def collect_render_records(
    store: FactsStore,
    property_id: str,
) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
    active = store.list_active_facts(property_id)
    active_fact_ids = {str(fact["fact_id"]) for fact in active}

    records: list[dict[str, object]] = [_with_status(fact, "active") for fact in active]

    pending = (
        fact
        for fact in store.list_facts()
        if str(fact["property_id"]) == property_id
        and fact["status"] == "pending"
        and str(fact["fact_id"]) not in active_fact_ids
    )
    records.extend(_with_status(fact, "pending") for fact in pending)
    return records


def _with_status(fact: Mapping[str, object], status: str) -> dict[str, object]:
    record = dict(fact)
    record["status"] = status
    return record


__all__: Iterable[str] = ("collect_render_records",)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated

from anyio.to_thread import run_sync
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from app.core.config import Settings, get_settings
from app.extract.models import Fact, FactStatus, SourceCitation
from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope
from app.schemas.ingest import IngestRequest, IngestResponse
from app.storage.facts import open_facts

router = APIRouter()


@router.post("", response_model=IngestResponse)
async def ingest_envelope(
    request: IngestRequest,
    settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)],
) -> IngestResponse:
    envelope_id = _envelope_id(request.envelope)
    fact = request.fact or _default_fact(request.envelope, envelope_id=envelope_id)

    def write_fact() -> tuple[str, str]:
        store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
        fact_id = store.upsert_fact(
            fact_id=fact.fact_id,
            property_id=fact.property_id,
            section=fact.section,
            key=fact.key,
            value=fact.value,
            status=fact.status.value,
            supersedes_fact_id=fact.supersedes_fact_id,
            asserted_at=fact.asserted_at,
            valid_from=fact.valid_interval.start,
            valid_to=fact.valid_interval.end,
            source_id=fact.citation.source_id,
            source_kind=fact.citation.source_kind,
            source_locator=fact.citation.locator,
            source_excerpt=fact.citation.quote,
            source_timestamp=fact.citation.source_timestamp,
            content_hash=fact.citation.payload_hash,
            extractor="ingest.minimum",
            confidence=fact.confidence,
            idempotency_key=f"fact:{envelope_id}",
        )
        event_id = store.append_event(
            "envelope.ingested",
            request.envelope.model_dump(mode="json"),
            fact_id=fact_id,
            idempotency_key=f"event:{envelope_id}",
        )
        return fact_id, event_id

    fact_id, event_id = await run_sync(write_fact)
    return IngestResponse(envelope_id=envelope_id, fact_id=fact_id, event_id=event_id)


def _envelope_id(envelope: Envelope) -> str:
    return f"env_{envelope.payload_hash[:32].lower()}"


def _default_fact(envelope: Envelope, *, envelope_id: str) -> Fact:
    return Fact(
        property_id=envelope.property,
        section="ingest",
        key=f"{envelope.kind}-{envelope.payload_hash[:12].lower()}",
        value={
            "envelope_id": envelope_id,
            "source": envelope.source,
            "kind": envelope.kind,
            "content_type": envelope.content_type,
            "payload_uri": envelope.payload_uri,
        },
        citation=SourceCitation(
            source_id=envelope.source,
            source_kind=envelope.kind,
            source_uri=envelope.payload_uri,
            locator=envelope.payload_uri or envelope.payload_hash[:16].lower(),
            payload_hash=envelope.payload_hash,
        ),
        confidence=1.0,
        status=FactStatus.ACTIVE,
        asserted_at=envelope.received_at,
    )

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated

from anyio.to_thread import run_sync
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from app.core.config import Settings, get_settings
from app.extract.models import Fact, FactStatus, SourceCitation
from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope
from app.render import collect_render_records, write_building_markdown
from app.schemas.ingest import IngestRequest, IngestResponse
from app.storage.facts import open_facts

router = APIRouter()


@router.post("", response_model=IngestResponse)
async def ingest_envelope(
    request: IngestRequest,
    settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)],
) -> IngestResponse:
    envelope_id = _envelope_id(request.envelope)
    fact = request.fact or _default_fact(request.envelope, envelope_id=envelope_id)

    def write_fact() -> tuple[str, str]:
        store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
        fact_id = store.upsert_fact(
            fact_id=fact.fact_id,
            property_id=fact.property_id,
            section=fact.section,
            key=fact.key,
            value=fact.value,
            status=fact.status.value,
            supersedes_fact_id=fact.supersedes_fact_id,
            asserted_at=fact.asserted_at,
            valid_from=fact.valid_interval.start,
            valid_to=fact.valid_interval.end,
            source_id=fact.citation.source_id,
            source_kind=fact.citation.source_kind,
            source_locator=fact.citation.locator,
            source_excerpt=fact.citation.quote,
            source_timestamp=fact.citation.source_timestamp,
            content_hash=fact.citation.payload_hash,
            extractor="ingest.minimum",
            confidence=fact.confidence,
            idempotency_key=f"fact:{envelope_id}",
        )
        event_id = store.append_event(
            "envelope.ingested",
            request.envelope.model_dump(mode="json"),
            fact_id=fact_id,
            idempotency_key=f"event:{envelope_id}",
        )
        return fact_id, event_id

    fact_id, event_id = await run_sync(write_fact)
    return IngestResponse(envelope_id=envelope_id, fact_id=fact_id, event_id=event_id)


def _envelope_id(envelope: Envelope) -> str:
    return f"env_{envelope.payload_hash[:32].lower()}"


def _default_fact(envelope: Envelope, *, envelope_id: str) -> Fact:
    return Fact(
        property_id=envelope.property,
        section="ingest",
        key=f"{envelope.kind}-{envelope.payload_hash[:12].lower()}",
        value={
            "envelope_id": envelope_id,
            "source": envelope.source,
            "kind": envelope.kind,
            "content_type": envelope.content_type,
            "payload_uri": envelope.payload_uri,
        },
        citation=SourceCitation(
            source_id=envelope.source,
            source_kind=envelope.kind,
            source_uri=envelope.payload_uri,
            locator=envelope.payload_uri or envelope.payload_hash[:16].lower(),
            payload_hash=envelope.payload_hash,
        ),
        confidence=1.0,
        status=FactStatus.ACTIVE,
        asserted_at=envelope.received_at,
    )

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import cast

from httpx import AsyncClient

from app.storage.facts import open_facts

PAYLOAD_HASH = "b" * 64


def _request_body(payload_hash: str = PAYLOAD_HASH) -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "envelope": {
            "source": "stammdaten",
            "kind": "json",
            "org": "org-001",
            "property": "LIE-001",
            "content_type": "application/json",
            "payload_uri": "data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json",
            "payload_hash": payload_hash,
            "received_at": "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z",
        }
    }


async def test_ingest_writes_one_fact_row(client: AsyncClient, settings) -> None:
    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["envelope_id"] == f"env_{PAYLOAD_HASH[:32]}"

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    facts = store.list_facts()
    events = store.list_events()

    assert len(facts) == 1
    assert facts[0]["fact_id"] == body["fact_id"]
    assert facts[0]["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert facts[0]["section"] == "ingest"
    assert facts[0]["source_kind"] == "json"
    assert [event["kind"] for event in events] == ["fact.asserted", "envelope.ingested"]
    envelope_event = next(event for event in events if event["kind"] == "envelope.ingested")
    assert envelope_event["event_id"] == body["event_id"]
    assert envelope_event["fact_id"] == body["fact_id"]


async def test_ingest_duplicate_payload_is_idempotent(client: AsyncClient, settings) -> None:
    first = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())
    second = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())

    assert first.status_code == 200
    assert second.status_code == 200
    assert second.json() == first.json()

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    assert len(store.list_facts()) == 1
    assert [event["kind"] for event in store.list_events()] == [
        "fact.asserted",
        "envelope.ingested",
    ]


async def test_ingest_rejects_bad_envelope_id(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    body = _request_body()
    envelope = cast(dict[str, object], body["envelope"])
    envelope["property"] = "../etc/passwd"

    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=body)

    assert response.status_code == 422

content.text:
Phase 2 done. Summary:

**Review findings + fix:**
- Phase 2 store auto-emits `fact.asserted` on insert. Old ingest test asserted `len(events)==1` (envelope.ingested only). Updated to expect `["fact.asserted", "envelope.ingested"]`.
- API/renderer consistency: `FactsStore.list_active_facts` overrides `current_status="active"` but row's stored `status` remains "pending" for activated facts. Renderer reads `status`, so without remapping, activated-pending facts would render as pending. Added `app/render/from_facts.py::collect_render_records` that re-stamps `status` per projection state.

**Wiring:**
- Ingest endpoint now: upsert fact → append envelope event → collect render records → write `output/{property_id}/building.md` (sync duckdb in `run_sync`, async file write after).
- New integration test: `test_ingest_renders_building_markdown_for_property` — verifies file exists with `# Building LIE-001`, active section, source citation.

**Verification:**
- `uv run pytest tests/storage/test_facts.py tests/engine/test_render.py tests/api/test_ingest.py` → 19 passed.
- `uv run ruff check .` → clean.
- `uv run pytest` → 162 passed (was 161).

**Status:** Phase 2 green. `ty check` pre-existing debt outside this slice not addressed. Ready for Phase 3 when you say go.

# Phase 0.5 Survivor Inventory

Date: 2026-05-04

Scope: inventory only. No files were moved or deleted. Status values are
`keep`, `move`, `delete`, and `blocks-on-phase-N`.

The main rule behind this inventory: preserve the current demo/wiki path until
the facts pipeline can ingest, render, and answer from `output/{property_id}/building.md`.
Delete generated runtime artifacts only after they are listed here.

## Summary

| Path | Status | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `data/` | keep | Read-only seed data. The refactor still depends on this as the source of truth. |
| `output/` | keep | Runtime write root remains in the target architecture. Contents below are old runtime survivors. |
| `wiki/` | blocks-on-phase-3 | Current markdown wiki, git audit repo, and demo query substrate. Delete after facts storage, render, and `property_de` replacement are proven. |

name: wiki-schema-principles
description: Architectural principles for the PropContext property wiki. Page-per-entity layout, four-op patcher contract, frontmatter discipline, alias-as-context principle. Loaded as the second half of the extractor system prompt. Keep short.
---

# Wiki Schema — Principles

PropContext's living-context wiki for German WEG property management follows the Karpathy `llm-wiki` pattern, adapted for event-driven ingestion. **Principles, not templates.** The LLM owns the body shape; the runtime owns the patch primitives and page index.

## 1. One property, one tree

Each Liegenschaft has its own subtree under `wiki/<LIE-id>/`. The runtime never crosses property boundaries.

## 2. Layered files

| File / dir | Owner | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `index.md` | runtime | Catalog of every page (regenerated each ingest) |
| `log.md` | LLM via `prepend_log` | Append-only chronological narrative |
| `building.md` | LLM | Synthesis / overview — the served headline |
| `entities/<ID>.md` | LLM | One page per canonical entity (DL, MIE, EIG, EH, HAUS, …) |
| `sources/<event_id>.md` | LLM | One page per ingested source |
| `topics/<slug>.md` | LLM | Issue / theme / project pages, created on demand |
| `lint_report.md` | runtime | Latest lint findings |

The LLM may invent new top-level dirs when a new kind of thing genuinely doesn't fit (`disputes/`, `meetings/`, `contracts/`). Reuse existing dirs first.

## 3. Frontmatter is the discovery layer

Every page begins with:

```yaml
---
name: <kebab-case>
description: <≤1024 chars; names entities, says when to read>
---
```

The next agent reads frontmatter only and decides whether to drill in. The runtime reads `name` + `description` to build `index.md`.

## 4. Four-op patcher contract

The LLM emits a `PatchPlan` with at most four kinds of op:

- `create_page(path, frontmatter, body)` — idempotent
- `upsert_section(path, heading, body)` — replace section body, append if missing
- `append_section(path, heading, line)` — append a line, create section if missing
- `prepend_log(line)` — prepend to `log.md`

The patcher is mechanical: zero LLM at apply time. Pure regex / line operations. Atomic write + git commit per event.

## 5. Provenance via footnotes

Every fact in a managed page carries a footnote pointing at its source: `[^EMAIL-12044]`. Footnote definitions live near the bottom of the page (heading at LLM's discretion — `## Provenance`, `## Sources`, …). The two-hop trace is `claim → footnote → sources/<event_id>.md → normalize/.../<event_id>.md`.

## 6. Wikilinks build the graph

Use `[[entities/DL-010.md]]` or short forms `[[DL-010]]` to cross-reference. The lint pass uses inbound-link counts to detect orphans.

## 7. Alias accumulation, not vocabulary control

There is no controlled vocabulary file. Variant references to the same entity (different names, email addresses, spellings) are **accumulated on the entity page** as the LLM encounters them. The wiki is its own dictionary.

The LLM uses context (`resolved_entities` from stammdaten + existing pages + located sections) to map mentions to canonical IDs — no hard validator.

## 8. Compactness

- Bullets > prose. Tables for repeating rows. Short paragraphs for synthesis only.
- Page soft target ≤30 KB; hard cap 50 KB triggers a split into child pages.
- Long timelines / chronological lists belong in `log.md` or a topic page, not in the entity page.

## 9. Human edits are sacred

Any page may contain a `# Human Notes` H1 followed by free-form human content. The patcher refuses to write past that boundary. The dedicated `PUT /api/v1/wiki/human-notes` endpoint owns that section.

## 10. Index, log, lint

- **index.md** is regenerated every ingest from page frontmatter. Don't patch it.
- **log.md** receives one line per event via `prepend_log`. Don't patch it directly.
- **lint_report.md** is rewritten each lint pass with orphan + missing-frontmatter findings.

## 11. Why this works

Karpathy's insight: maintenance is the bottleneck of a knowledge base, not capture. By making the patch primitives mechanical and the layout LLM-owned, we get a graph that compounds with every ingest. The human reads; the LLM writes; git remembers; the wiki keeps getting denser.
---STAMMDATEN---
{
  "liegenschaft": {
    "id": "LIE-001",
    "name": "WEG Immanuelkirchstraße 26",
    "strasse": "Immanuelkirchstraße 26",
    "plz": "10405",
    "ort": "Berlin",

    "verwalter": "Huber & Partner Immobilienverwaltung GmbH",

    "verwalter_ort": "Berlin",
    "verwalter_email": "info@huber-partner-verwaltung.de",
    "verwalter_telefon": "+49 30 12345-0",

    "verwalter_bic": "COBADEFFXXX",

    "weg_bankkonto_bank": "Postbank Berlin",
    "ruecklage_iban": "DE12 1203 0000 0098 7654 32",

  },
  "gebaeude": [

README.md
---
---
name: wiki-schema-principles
description: Architectural principles for the PropContext property wiki. Page-per-entity layout, four-op patcher contract, frontmatter discipline, alias-as-context principle. Loaded as the second half of the extractor system prompt. Keep short.
---

# Wiki Schema — Principles

PropContext's living-context wiki for German WEG property management follows the Karpathy `llm-wiki` pattern, adapted for event-driven ingestion. **Principles, not templates.** The LLM owns the body shape; the runtime owns the patch primitives and page index.

## 1. One property, one tree

Each Liegenschaft has its own subtree under `wiki/<LIE-id>/`. The runtime never crosses property boundaries.

## 2. Layered files

| File / dir | Owner | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `index.md` | runtime | Catalog of every page (regenerated each ingest) |
| `log.md` | LLM via `prepend_log` | Append-only chronological narrative |
| `building.md` | LLM | Synthesis / overview — the served headline |
| `entities/<ID>.md` | LLM | One page per canonical entity (DL, MIE, EIG, EH, HAUS, …) |
| `sources/<event_id>.md` | LLM | One page per ingested source |
| `topics/<slug>.md` | LLM | Issue / theme / project pages, created on demand |
| `lint_report.md` | runtime | Latest lint findings |

The LLM may invent new top-level dirs when a new kind of thing genuinely doesn't fit (`disputes/`, `meetings/`, `contracts/`). Reuse existing dirs first.

## 3. Frontmatter is the discovery layer

Every page begins with:

```yaml
---
name: <kebab-case>
description: <≤1024 chars; names entities, says when to read>
---
```

The next agent reads frontmatter only and decides whether to drill in. The runtime reads `name` + `description` to build `index.md`.

## 4. Four-op patcher contract

The LLM emits a `PatchPlan` with at most four kinds of op:

- `create_page(path, frontmatter, body)` — idempotent
- `upsert_section(path, heading, body)` — replace section body, append if missing
- `append_section(path, heading, line)` — append a line, create section if missing
- `prepend_log(line)` — prepend to `log.md`

The patcher is mechanical: zero LLM at apply time. Pure regex / line operations. Atomic write + git commit per event.

## 5. Provenance via footnotes

Every fact in a managed page carries a footnote pointing at its source: `[^EMAIL-12044]`. Footnote definitions live near the bottom of the page (heading at LLM's discretion — `## Provenance`, `## Sources`, …). The two-hop trace is `claim → footnote → sources/<event_id>.md → normalize/.../<event_id>.md`.

## 6. Wikilinks build the graph

Use `[[entities/DL-010.md]]` or short forms `[[DL-010]]` to cross-reference. The lint pass uses inbound-link counts to detect orphans.

## 7. Alias accumulation, not vocabulary control

There is no controlled vocabulary file. Variant references to the same entity (different names, email addresses, spellings) are **accumulated on the entity page** as the LLM encounters them. The wiki is its own dictionary.

The LLM uses context (`resolved_entities` from stammdaten + existing pages + located sections) to map mentions to canonical IDs — no hard validator.

## 8. Compactness

- Bullets > prose. Tables for repeating rows. Short paragraphs for synthesis only.
- Page soft target ≤30 KB; hard cap 50 KB triggers a split into child pages.
- Long timelines / chronological lists belong in `log.md` or a topic page, not in the entity page.

## 9. Human edits are sacred

Any page may contain a `# Human Notes` H1 followed by free-form human content. The patcher refuses to write past that boundary. The dedicated `PUT /api/v1/wiki/human-notes` endpoint owns that section.

## 10. Index, log, lint

- **index.md** is regenerated every ingest from page frontmatter. Don't patch it.
- **log.md** receives one line per event via `prepend_log`. Don't patch it directly.
- **lint_report.md** is rewritten each lint pass with orphan + missing-frontmatter findings.

## 11. Why this works

Karpathy's insight: maintenance is the bottleneck of a knowledge base, not capture. By making the patch primitives mechanical and the layout LLM-owned, we get a graph that compounds with every ingest. The human reads; the LLM writes; git remembers; the wiki keeps getting denser.

content.text:
Phase 3 is too big to land as one commit. Two real blockers to surface BEFORE you start cutting:

**Conflict to resolve with user first:**
- Plan §Phase 3 says delete `wiki_chunks.py` + reindex/locate in this phase's cleanup commit.
- `docs/phase0_inventory.md` marks `app/mcp/tools.py`, `app/mcp/resources.py`, `app/api/v1/wiki.py`, `app/api/v1/ask.py`, `app/services/wiki.py`, `app/services/locate.py`, `app/services/reindex.py` as `blocks-on-phase-3` or `blocks-on-phase-4`. They depend on `wiki_chunks`.
- If you delete `wiki_chunks` now, MCP tools/resources and the ask/wiki APIs break until Phase 4 fixes them. Either defer the cleanup commit until Phase 4, or accept a broken intermediate state. Ask the user which — don't pick silently.

**Stale-doc check before "moving" them:**
- CLAUDE.md and the plan reference `schema/VOCABULARY.md` and `schema/HERMES_LOOP.md`. Your `ls schema/` showed neither — only `CLAUDE.md`, `LEGAL_MAP.md`, `WIKI_SCHEMA.md`, `extractors/`. Verify with `ls schema/` and `rg "VOCABULARY|HERMES_LOOP" -l` before treating them as inputs to move.

**Suggested first slice (smallest landable cut, additive only):**
1. Scaffold `app/verticals/property_de/{plugin.py, orgs.py, observers.py, context/}`. Copy (don't move) `WIKI_SCHEMA.md` + `LEGAL_MAP.md` into `context/`. Engine boundary: nothing under `app/{core,api,mcp,ingest,parsers,llm,extract,classify,skills,storage,patcher,render,hermes}` may import from `app.verticals`.
2. Build one deterministic skill: stammdaten → ExtractionResult (facts for liegenschaft, gebäude, einheiten). Build fresh against `data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json` shape — do NOT port the 424-line `app/storage/stammdaten.py`; that's wiki-era logic. Skills are async, Pydantic-in/Pydantic-out per `app/skills/base.py::SkillRegistration`.
3. Tests under `tests/verticals/property_de/`. The plan's guardrail rg-grep wants engine modules clean of vertical imports — your test layout should match.
4. Move org allowlist last in this slice: replace `app/mcp/orgs.py` body with a thin wrapper that reads from the active vertical plugin, OR keep `app/mcp/orgs.py` as the indirection and have the vertical register into it. Either way, don't touch `app/mcp/context.py`.

Defer to next slice: bank/CAMT skill, classifier prompts, observers. Defer to Phase 4 (or end-of-Phase-3 only with user OK): wiki_chunks deletion.

**Parallelizable now:**
- Reading `app/services/handlers/bank.py` + `app/services/normalize/bank.py` + a sample of `data/bank/` (only to understand input shape, not to port logic).
- Verifying VOCABULARY/HERMES_LOOP existence.
- Checking what `data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json` fields you'll surface as facts.

Run those in parallel, then come back with the wiki_chunks question and proposed slice 1 scope before you write any new code.

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from functools import partial

import anyio

from app.core.config import Settings
from app.schemas.webhook import IngestEvent
from app.services.handlers.base import HandlerResult, PayloadMarkdownHandler
from app.services.normalize.bank import normalize_bank_row


class BankHandler(PayloadMarkdownHandler):
    kind = "bank"

    async def handle(self, event: IngestEvent, settings: Settings) -> HandlerResult:
        row = event.payload.get("row")
        if not isinstance(row, dict):
            return await super().handle(event, settings)
        document = await asyncio.to_thread(
            partial(
                normalize_bank_row,
                row,
                settings.normalize_dir,
                source=str(event.source_path or "webhook"),
            )
        )
        text = await anyio.Path(document.output_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        return HandlerResult(
            normalized_path=document.output_path,
            normalized_text=text,
            source_id=event.event_id,
            event_type=event.event_type,
        )

from __future__ import annotations

from app.verticals.property_de import build_plugin
from app.verticals.property_de.plugin import context_path


def test_plugin_loads_and_owns_org_allowlist() -> None:
    plugin = build_plugin()

    assert plugin.name == "property-de"
    assert plugin.allows("org_demo_berlin", "LIE-001")
    assert not plugin.allows("org_demo_berlin", "LIE-999")
    assert not plugin.allows("org_demo_hamburg", "LIE-001")


def test_context_files_resolve_to_packaged_resources() -> None:
    schema = context_path("WIKI_SCHEMA.md")
    legal = context_path("LEGAL_MAP.md")

    assert schema.is_file()
    assert legal.is_file()
    assert schema.read_text(encoding="utf-8").startswith("---")
    assert "WEG" in legal.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from collections.abc import Iterable, Mapping
from typing import Any

from pydantic import AwareDatetime, BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, JsonValue

from app.extract.models import (
    ExtractionResult,
    Fact,
    FactStatus,
    GapSignal,
    Payload,
    SourceCitation,
)
from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope, Identifier, UriString

SKILL_NAME = "parse-stammdaten"
SOURCE_KIND: Identifier = "stammdaten"


class StammdatenInput(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    payload: JsonValue
    source_id: Identifier
    source_uri: UriString | None = None
    source_timestamp: AwareDatetime | None = None


async def parse_stammdaten(skill_input: StammdatenInput) -> ExtractionResult:
    payload = skill_input.payload
    if not isinstance(payload, Mapping):
        return ExtractionResult(
            gap_signals=[
                GapSignal(
                    property_id="LIE-unknown",
                    section="property",
                    kind="unsupported",
                    message="stammdaten payload is not an object",
                    source_id=skill_input.source_id,
                )
            ]
        )

    liegenschaft = _as_mapping(payload.get("liegenschaft"))
    if liegenschaft is None or not isinstance(liegenschaft.get("id"), str):
        return ExtractionResult(
            gap_signals=[
                GapSignal(
                    property_id="LIE-unknown",
                    section="property",
                    kind="missing",
                    message="stammdaten missing liegenschaft.id",

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from collections.abc import Iterable, Mapping
from typing import Any

from pydantic import AwareDatetime, BaseModel, ConfigDict, JsonValue

from app.extract.models import (
    ExtractionResult,
    Fact,
    FactStatus,
    GapSignal,
    Payload,
    SourceCitation,
)
from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope, Identifier, UriString

SKILL_NAME = "parse-stammdaten"
SOURCE_KIND: Identifier = "stammdaten"


class StammdatenInput(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    payload: JsonValue
    source_id: Identifier
    source_uri: UriString | None = None
    source_timestamp: AwareDatetime | None = None


async def parse_stammdaten(skill_input: StammdatenInput) -> ExtractionResult:
    payload = skill_input.payload
    if not isinstance(payload, Mapping):
        return ExtractionResult(
            gap_signals=[
                GapSignal(
                    property_id="LIE-unknown",
                    section="property",
                    kind="unsupported",
                    message="stammdaten payload is not an object",
                    source_id=skill_input.source_id,
                )
            ]
        )

    liegenschaft = _as_mapping(payload.get("liegenschaft"))
    if liegenschaft is None or not isinstance(liegenschaft.get("id"), str):
        return ExtractionResult(
            gap_signals=[
                GapSignal(
                    property_id="LIE-unknown",
                    section="property",
                    kind="missing",
                    message="stammdaten missing liegenschaft.id",
                    source_id=skill_input.source_id,
                )
            ]
        )

    property_id: Identifier = str(liegenschaft["id"])
    facts: list[Fact] = []
    facts.append(_property_overview_fact(property_id, liegenschaft, skill_input))
    facts.extend(_manager_facts(property_id, liegenschaft, skill_input))
    facts.extend(_finance_facts(property_id, liegenschaft, skill_input))
    facts.extend(_building_facts(property_id, payload.get("gebaeude"), skill_input))
    facts.extend(_unit_facts(property_id, payload.get("einheiten"), skill_input))
    return ExtractionResult(facts=facts)


def _property_overview_fact(
    property_id: str,
    liegenschaft: Mapping[str, Any],
    skill_input: StammdatenInput,
) -> Fact:
    return _build_fact(
        property_id=property_id,
        section="property",
        key="overview",
        value={
            "id": property_id,
            "name": liegenschaft.get("name"),
            "strasse": liegenschaft.get("strasse"),
            "plz": liegenschaft.get("plz"),
            "ort": liegenschaft.get("ort"),
            "baujahr": liegenschaft.get("baujahr"),
            "sanierung": liegenschaft.get("sanierung"),
        },
        locator="/liegenschaft",
        skill_input=skill_input,
    )


def _manager_facts(
    property_id: str,
    liegenschaft: Mapping[str, Any],
    skill_input: StammdatenInput,
) -> list[Fact]:
    name = liegenschaft.get("verwalter")
    if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
        return []
    value = {
        "name": name,
        "strasse": liegenschaft.get("verwalter_strasse"),
        "plz": liegenschaft.get("verwalter_plz"),
        "ort": liegenschaft.get("verwalter_ort"),
        "email": liegenschaft.get("verwalter_email"),
        "telefon": liegenschaft.get("verwalter_telefon"),
        "steuernummer": liegenschaft.get("verwalter_steuernummer"),
    }
    return [
        _build_fact(
            property_id=property_id,
            section="contacts",
            key="manager",
            value=value,
            locator="/liegenschaft/verwalter",
            skill_input=skill_input,
        )
    ]


def _finance_facts(
    property_id: str,
    liegenschaft: Mapping[str, Any],
    skill_input: StammdatenInput,
) -> list[Fact]:
    accounts: tuple[tuple[str, str, str, str, str], ...] = (
        ("iban_weg", "weg_bankkonto_iban", "weg_bankkonto_bic", "weg_bankkonto_bank", "weg"),
        (
            "iban_ruecklage",
            "ruecklage_iban",
            "ruecklage_bic",
            "ruecklage_bank",
            "ruecklage",
        ),
        ("iban_verwalter", "verwalter_iban", "verwalter_bic", "verwalter_bank", "verwalter"),
    )
    facts: list[Fact] = []
    for key, iban_field, bic_field, bank_field, role in accounts:
        iban = liegenschaft.get(iban_field)
        if not isinstance(iban, str) or not iban.strip():
            continue
        facts.append(
            _build_fact(
                property_id=property_id,
                section="finance",
                key=key,
                value={
                    "iban": _normalize_iban(iban),
                    "bic": liegenschaft.get(bic_field),
                    "bank": liegenschaft.get(bank_field),
                    "role": role,
                },
                locator=f"/liegenschaft/{iban_field}",
                skill_input=skill_input,
            )
        )
    return facts


def _building_facts(
    property_id: str,
    raw: object,
    skill_input: StammdatenInput,
) -> list[Fact]:
    if not isinstance(raw, list):
        return []
    facts: list[Fact] = []
    for index, entry in enumerate(raw):
        building = _as_mapping(entry)
        if building is None:
            continue
        building_id = building.get("id")
        if not isinstance(building_id, str):
            continue
        facts.append(
            _build_fact(
                property_id=property_id,
                section="buildings",
                key=str(building_id),
                value=dict(building),
                locator=f"/gebaeude/{index}",
                skill_input=skill_input,
            )
        )
    return facts


def _unit_facts(
    property_id: str,
    raw: object,
    skill_input: StammdatenInput,
) -> list[Fact]:
    if not isinstance(raw, list):
        return []
    facts: list[Fact] = []
    for index, entry in enumerate(raw):
        unit = _as_mapping(entry)
        if unit is None:
            continue
        unit_id = unit.get("id")
        if not isinstance(unit_id, str):
            continue
        facts.append(
            _build_fact(
                property_id=property_id,
                section="units",
                key=str(unit_id),
                value=dict(unit),
                locator=f"/einheiten/{index}",
                skill_input=skill_input,
            )
        )
    return facts


def _build_fact(
    *,
    property_id: str,
    section: str,
    key: str,
    value: Mapping[str, Any] | str | int | float | bool | None,
    locator: str,
    skill_input: StammdatenInput,
) -> Fact:
    return Fact(
        property_id=property_id,
        section=section,
        key=key,
        value=_json_compatible(value),
        citation=SourceCitation(
            source_id=skill_input.source_id,
            source_kind=SOURCE_KIND,
            source_uri=skill_input.source_uri,
            locator=locator,
            source_timestamp=skill_input.source_timestamp,
        ),
        confidence=1.0,
        status=FactStatus.ACTIVE,
    )


def _json_compatible(value: Any) -> JsonValue:
    return json.loads(json.dumps(value, default=str))


def _as_mapping(value: object) -> Mapping[str, Any] | None:
    return value if isinstance(value, Mapping) else None


def _normalize_iban(value: str) -> str:
    return "".join(value.split()).upper()


class StammdatenExtractor:
    name = SKILL_NAME

    async def extract(self, envelope: Envelope, payload: Payload) -> ExtractionResult:
        decoded = _decode_payload(payload)
        skill_input = StammdatenInput(
            payload=decoded,
            source_id=envelope.source,
            source_uri=envelope.payload_uri,
            source_timestamp=envelope.received_at,
        )
        return await parse_stammdaten(skill_input)


def _decode_payload(payload: Payload) -> JsonValue:
    if isinstance(payload, (bytes, bytearray)):
        return json.loads(bytes(payload).decode("utf-8"))
    if isinstance(payload, str):
        return json.loads(payload)
    return payload  # type: ignore[return-value]


__all__: Iterable[str] = (
    "SKILL_NAME",
    "StammdatenExtractor",
    "StammdatenInput",
    "parse_stammdaten",
)
