# PropContext - Implementation Plan

Greenfield refactor to a vertical-agnostic engine with a bundled
`property_de` vertical. Fork-and-run single repo, Apache-2.0, demoed against a
German WEG/property-operator workflow.

Core thesis: the engine ingests, extracts, stores, renders, and serves context.
The vertical supplies domain context: schema, vocabulary, prompts, examples,
render template, org allowlist, and optional deterministic skills. Adding a new
vertical should mostly mean adding a new context bundle, not rewriting engine
code.

---

## Value To Preserve

- One repo that a user can fork and run without publishing a library.
- `data/` remains read-only seed data; `output/` owns all runtime writes.
- Evidence stays first-class: every fact has source URI, locator, confidence,
  valid time, and transaction time.
- LLM-first extraction handles messy emails, invoices, letters, and bookings.
- Deterministic skills cover rigid formats where LLMs are wasteful or risky:
  stammdaten JSON, bank/CAMT data, dates, IBANs, EUR amounts.
- MCP is the main query surface for external AI agents.
- Hermes remains a gated feedback substrate: collect gaps and query misses,
  produce proposals, never auto-apply.

---

## Locked Decisions

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| Distribution | Single fork-and-run repo. No PyPI package. |
| Compatibility | Greenfield delete is allowed. No backward compatibility layer. |
| Shape | Engine modules under `app/`; verticals under `app/verticals/`. |
| Default vertical | `app/verticals/property_de/`. |
| Storage | Replace `wiki_chunks.duckdb` with `facts.duckdb` containing facts and events. |
| Audit model | Bitemporal facts plus append-only events. No git-per-event flow. |
| Fixture ID | Read dynamic ID from `data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json`, not hardcoded `LIE-001`. |
| Auth MVP | Hardcoded org-to-property allowlist in the property vertical. |
| LLM models | Settings-driven classify/extract model names. Do not bake model IDs into code. |
| Self-improvement | Manual proposal review only. No Hermes auto-patching. |
| Demo ops | Docker verification is post-demo polish, not part of MVP DoD. |

---

## Target Shape

Keep the final tree compact. Avoid creating one module per idea until there is
real behavior behind it.

```text
app/
  core/                 config, logging, app factory helpers
  api/                  FastAPI routers, including ingest and health
  mcp/                  MCP server, auth, context, tools, resources
  ingest/               envelope, adapters, routing, idempotency
  parsers/              bytes to text/structured primitives
  llm/                  async LLM client and tool-calling glue
  extract/              Extractor protocol, Fact models, LLMExtractor
  classify/             Classifier protocol, LLMClassifier
  skills/               Skill protocol, registry, builtin deterministic helpers
  storage/              facts, events, db helpers
  patcher/              fact upsert/supersede logic
  render/               facts to building.md
  hermes/               gap/query-miss aggregation
  plugin.py             VerticalPlugin protocol
  main.py               create_app(active_plugin)
  verticals/
    property_de/
      plugin.py
      context/          schema, vocabulary, legal map, prompts, examples
      skills/           parse_stammdaten, parse_camt053
      observers.py
      orgs.py
      template.md

tests/
  engine/
  verticals/property_de/
  e2e/

scripts/
  reset.sh
  ingest_fixture.py
  query_demo.py
  hermes_aggregate.py
```

---

## Core Contracts

These contracts are the value of the refactor. Keep them stable and small.

- `Envelope`: source, kind, org, property, content type, payload URI/hash,
  received time, valid time hint, extensions.
- `Fact`: property ID, section, key, JSON value, source URI, source locator,
  confidence, status, valid interval, transaction interval, supersession link.
- `ExtractionResult`: facts plus gap signals.
- `Extractor`: async `extract(envelope, payload) -> ExtractionResult`.
- `Skill`: deterministic async callable shaped as
  `async (input: BaseModel) -> BaseModel`. Adapters expose a skill as an
  `Extractor` or an LLM tool. The registry stores the skill plus adapter type.
- `VerticalPlugin`: name, classifier, extractor registry, skills, render
  template, observers, auth allowlist.
- `EventLog`: append and replay events for fact upserts, supersedes, gap
  signals, query misses, and owner notes.

---

## Guardrails

- Engine code must never import `app.verticals.*`.

  ```bash
  ! rg "from app\.verticals|import app\.verticals" \
      app/core app/api app/mcp app/ingest app/parsers app/llm \
      app/extract app/classify app/skills app/storage app/patcher \
      app/render app/hermes app/plugin.py
  ```

- Vertical code may import engine protocols.
- Routes and IO services stay async. No blocking file/network work inside async
  functions without `anyio` or a thread handoff.
- Keep LLM clients behind protocols and inject fakes in tests.
- Use Pydantic v2 models at boundaries. Validate IDs before filesystem access.
- Do not add new dependencies while implementing this plan without a short
  comparison and human sign-off.
- Leave old wiki-chunk services untouched but unused until the facts pipeline and
  `property_de` vertical are proven, then delete them in one Phase 3 cleanup
  commit.

---

## Performance Budget

Fail loudly when a run exceeds these budgets. Do not silently degrade into slow
or expensive behavior.

| Budget | Limit |
|---|---:|
| Envelopes per ingest run | 50 |
| LLM tokens per extraction | 8k |
| Wall time per envelope | 10s |

---

## Build Sequence

### Phase 0 - Cleanup

Goal: remove stale paths and make the repo ready for the new architecture.

Build:

- Move stale docs into `docs/archive/`.
- Add Apache-2.0 `LICENSE` if missing.
- Ensure `output/` is gitignored except `.gitkeep`.
- Create branch `refactor/vertical-engine`.

Done:

- `git status` shows only intentional cleanup.
- `data/` is untouched.

### Phase 0.5 - Survivor Inventory

Goal: make deletion decisions visible before the greenfield cleanup starts.

Build:

- List every path under `wiki/`, `output/`, `app/services/handlers/`, and other
  stale runtime/demo areas discovered during cleanup.
- Mark each path as `keep`, `move`, `delete`, or `blocks-on-phase-N`.
- Commit the inventory as `docs/phase0_inventory.md`.
- Treat the inventory as a one-shot reference doc and delete it after Phase 6.

Done:

- `docs/phase0_inventory.md` exists and covers every survivor path.
- No runtime data is deleted before it appears in the inventory.

### Phase 1 - Engine, Ingest, Storage Minimum

Goal: establish the engine contracts and ingestion path without vertical
business logic, with a real DuckDB sink.

Build:

- Write `docs/adr/0001-bitemporal-facts.md` before facts storage code in this
  phase. It blocks storage work and Phase 2. The ADR must decide: supersede
  creates a new row and closes the prior transaction interval without SQL
  `UPDATE`; pending-to-active is represented by an event, not row mutation; v1
  `as_of` means transaction time only; valid-time as-of is deferred; one active
  row per `(property_id, section, key)` is enforced by a partial unique index or
  an equivalent DuckDB-supported invariant.
- Add `Envelope`, `Fact`, `ExtractionResult`, `Extractor`, `Skill`, and
  `VerticalPlugin`.
- Create `facts.duckdb` with minimum `facts` and `events` tables.
- Add `EventLog.emit(kind, payload)`.
- Add async LLM client protocol plus a fake test client.
- Emit `gap_signal` through `EventLog` from LLM extraction.
- Add parsers for the currently used payload types: email, PDF/text fallback,
  JSON, and CSV/bank rows.
- Add ingest adapter/router with payload hash idempotency.
- Add bare fact upsert. No supersede/as-of/render behavior yet.
- Add built-in deterministic helpers for German dates, EUR amounts, IBANs, and
  addresses.

Tests:

- Envelope validation and bad ID rejection.
- Ingest idempotency.
- Facts schema initializes and accepts one upserted row.
- `EventLog.emit` stores an append-only event.
- Skill registry schema generation and dispatch.
- LLM extractor with mocked structured response and tool call.

Done:

- `POST /api/v1/ingest` accepts a fixture payload, returns an envelope ID, and
  writes at least one fact row.
- No engine module imports a vertical.

### Phase 2 - Supersede, As-Of, Render

Goal: turn the minimum fact sink into a usable bitemporal projection.

Build:

- Implement active, by-section, and as-of queries.
- Implement patcher upsert/supersede behavior with event emission.
- Render `output/{property_id}/building.md` from active and pending facts.

Tests:

- Upsert, duplicate noop, supersede, status transitions.
- As-of query returns a frozen snapshot.
- Event replay reconstructs current facts.
- Render includes source links and pending questions.

Done:

- `docs/adr/0001-bitemporal-facts.md` exists and Phase 2 behavior follows it.
- Fixture facts can be stored, queried, and rendered into `building.md`.

### Phase 3 - Property-DE Vertical

Goal: move domain knowledge out of engine code.

Build:

- Move or convert `schema/WIKI_SCHEMA.md` and `schema/LEGAL_MAP.md`
  into `app/verticals/property_de/context/`. Vocabulary lives inline in the
  vertical's observers (no controlled-vocab file; see WIKI_SCHEMA §7).
- Add prompts and examples for classify, email, invoice, letter, booking.
- Rewrite stammdaten and bank/CAMT handlers as deterministic skills.
- Move org allowlist into the vertical.
- Register extractors in `property_de/plugin.py`: LLM for messy documents,
  deterministic skills for rigid inputs.
- Add vertical observers that emit `vocab_gap` and `legal_gap` events.
- Delete `wiki_chunks.py`, reindex/locate services, old wiki index paths, and
  related tests in one cleanup commit:
  `chore: remove wiki_chunks now that facts pipeline is live`.

Tests:

- Context files load and parse.
- Stammdaten fixture creates expected core facts.
- Bank fixture creates expected transaction facts.
- Plugin registers every supported kind.
- Vertical observers emit expected gap events from seeded cases.

Done:

- `app/services/handlers/` is gone.
- All property-specific state lives under `app/verticals/property_de/`.
- Tests do not hardcode `LIE-001` except where the fixture itself contains it.
- `rg wiki_chunks app tests scripts` returns nothing.

### Phase 4 - API and MCP Surface

Goal: make the new fact store useful to external agents.

Build:

- Keep health routes simple.
- Add or rewrite read APIs around facts and rendered markdown.
- Implement MCP tools:
  `get_active_facts`, `get_pending_questions`, `get_obligations`,
  `time_travel`, `add_owner_note`, `cite`.
- Implement MCP resources:
  `property://{id}`, `building://{org}/{property}/active`,
  `building://{org}/{property}/pending`.
- Enforce org/property access through shared MCP context helpers.
- Emit `query_miss` events from empty API/MCP query paths.

Tests:

- MCP handshake and tool listing.
- Cross-org access denied.
- `cite` returns source URI and locator for a fact.
- Golden Q&A pack returns sourced answers.

Done:

- `scripts/query_demo.py` prints at least 5 sourced answers over MCP.
- Every answer can be traced to one or more facts.

### Phase 5 - Hermes and Demo

Goal: prove the self-improvement claim without adding autonomous mutation.

Build:

- Aggregate recent `gap_signal`, `query_miss`, `vocab_gap`, and `legal_gap`
  events into
  `output/hermes/proposals.md`.
- Add demo scripts: reset, fixture ingest, query demo, Hermes aggregate.
- Update README quickstart.

Tests:

- Aggregator groups seeded gaps and query misses.
- Property vertical observers score vocabulary/legal-map gaps.
- Demo scripts work against `tmp_path` or isolated output dirs where practical.

Done:

- Fresh reset plus fixture ingest creates `facts.duckdb`, `building.md`, and
  queryable MCP results.
- Hermes aggregation writes at least one proposal block after seeded events.

### Phase 6 - Proof and Polish

Goal: validate the extensibility story, then stop.

Build:

- Add one fictional Arbio booking JSON example by prompt/example/plugin
  registration only.
- Add CI import guard for engine-to-vertical isolation.
- Update `AGENTS.md` with the new patterns:
  adding a vertical, adding an ingest kind, adding a deterministic skill,
  bitemporal status meanings, Hermes review flow.

Done:

- Booking kind ingests without engine changes.
- After the Arbio commit, `git diff main -- app/ ':!app/verticals/'` is empty.
  CI runs this as a separate extensibility-claim job.
- `AGENTS.md` and README describe the architecture users will actually see.
- `uv run ruff format --check`, `uv run ruff check`, `uv run ty check`, and
  `uv run pytest` pass.

---

## Cut Lines

If time is tight, keep the demo path intact and cut polish.

Must ship:

- Fixture ingest to `facts.duckdb`.
- Rendered `output/{property_id}/building.md`.
- MCP query with sourced answers.
- Cross-org access denial.
- No `wiki_chunks` dependency.

Can defer:

- Arbio booking proof.
- Owner notes.
- Full Docker polish.
- Real LLM integration tests in CI.
- Large golden pack beyond 5 representative questions.

Do not cut:

- Source locators.
- Bitemporal fields.
- Dynamic fixture property ID.
- Engine/vertical import boundary.
- Fake LLM test path.

---

## Commit Sequence

1. `chore: clean stale docs and runtime artifacts`
2. `docs: inventory survivor paths`
3. `docs: record bitemporal facts ADR`
4. `feat: add engine contracts and facts sink`
5. `feat: render building markdown from facts`
6. `feat: add property_de vertical plugin and skills`
7. `chore: remove wiki_chunks now that facts pipeline is live`
8. `feat: expose facts through API and MCP`
9. `feat: add Hermes proposal aggregation`
10. `test: add fixture ingest and MCP golden coverage`
11. `docs: document vertical-engine architecture`
12. `ci: enforce engine import boundary and Arbio diff guard`

---

## Risks

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| LLM cost grows with fixture size | Payload-hash idempotency; classify/extract split; keep demo fixture bounded. |
| LLM hallucination | Structured output, required source locator, confidence threshold to pending. |
| Ingest cost or latency blows up | Enforce the performance budget table and fail loudly above thresholds. |
| Refactor touches many files | Delete obsolete paths early; keep phases independently revertable. |
| Vertical boundary leaks | CI grep plus tests that import engine without loading vertical. |
| As-of semantics get complex | Keep facts table simple: active row plus superseded history with txn intervals. |
| Hermes overpromises | README says proposals are generated for human review only. |

---

## Definition Of Done

1. `rg wiki_chunks app tests scripts` returns nothing.
2. Engine import guard passes.
3. `rg "LIE-001" app tests` returns nothing except explicit fixture data.
4. `app/services/handlers/` does not exist.
5. `uv run pytest` passes.
6. `scripts/ingest_fixture.py` populates `output/facts.duckdb`.
7. `output/{property_id}/building.md` renders active and pending facts with
   source links.
8. `scripts/query_demo.py` prints sourced MCP answers.
9. `scripts/hermes_aggregate.py` writes `output/hermes/proposals.md`.
10. Adding a new ingest kind requires prompt/example/plugin registration, not
    engine changes.
11. The Arbio CI guard proves that the sample ingest kind changed only vertical
    files.

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated

from anyio.to_thread import run_sync
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from app.core.config import Settings, get_settings
from app.extract.models import ExtractionResult, Fact, FactStatus, GapSignal, SourceCitation
from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope
from app.plugin import ObservedEvent, VerticalPlugin, get_active_plugin
from app.render import collect_render_records, write_building_markdown
from app.schemas.ingest import IngestRequest, IngestResponse
from app.storage.facts import FactsStore, open_facts

router = APIRouter()


@router.post("", response_model=IngestResponse)
async def ingest_envelope(
    request: IngestRequest,
    settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)],
) -> IngestResponse:
    plugin = get_active_plugin()
    envelope_id = _envelope_id(request.envelope)
    extraction = await _extract(request, plugin, envelope_id)
    facts = list(extraction.facts)
    gap_signals = list(extraction.gap_signals)
    observations = await _collect_observations(plugin, facts)
    property_id = facts[0].property_id if facts else request.envelope.property

    def commit() -> tuple[str, list[str], list[str], list[str], list[dict[str, object]]]:
        store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
        fact_ids = [
            _upsert_fact(store, fact, envelope_id=envelope_id, index=index)
            for index, fact in enumerate(facts)
        ]
        gap_event_ids = [
            _emit_gap_event(store, signal, envelope_id=envelope_id, index=index)
            for index, signal in enumerate(gap_signals)
        ]
        observation_event_ids = [
            _emit_observed_event(store, observed, envelope_id=envelope_id, index=index)
            for index, observed in enumerate(observations)
        ]
        envelope_event_id = store.append_event(
            "envelope.ingested",
            request.envelope.model_dump(mode="json"),
            fact_id=fact_ids[0] if fact_ids else None,
            idempotency_key=f"event:{envelope_id}",
        )
        records = collect_render_records(store, property_id)
        return envelope_event_id, fact_ids, gap_event_ids, observation_event_ids, records

    (
        envelope_event_id,
        fact_ids,
        gap_event_ids,
        observation_event_ids,
        records,
    ) = await run_sync(commit)
    await write_building_markdown(settings.output_dir, property_id, records)
    return IngestResponse(
        envelope_id=envelope_id,
        event_id=envelope_event_id,
        fact_ids=fact_ids,
        gap_event_ids=gap_event_ids,
        observation_event_ids=observation_event_ids,
    )


async def _collect_observations(
    plugin: VerticalPlugin,
    facts: list[Fact],
) -> list[ObservedEvent]:
    if not plugin.observers or not facts:
        return []
    observed: list[ObservedEvent] = []
    for observer in plugin.observers:
        for fact in facts:
            for event in await observer.observe("fact.extracted", fact):
                observed.append(event)
    return observed


async def _extract(
    request: IngestRequest,
    plugin: VerticalPlugin,
    envelope_id: str,
) -> ExtractionResult:
    if request.fact is not None:
        return ExtractionResult(facts=[request.fact])

    if request.payload is not None:
        extractor = plugin.extractors.get(request.envelope.kind)
        if extractor is not None:
            return await extractor.extract(request.envelope, request.payload)

    return ExtractionResult(facts=[_default_fact(request.envelope, envelope_id=envelope_id)])


def _envelope_id(envelope: Envelope) -> str:
    return f"env_{envelope.payload_hash[:32].lower()}"


def _upsert_fact(
    store: FactsStore,
    fact: Fact,
    *,
    envelope_id: str,
    index: int,
) -> str:
    return store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id=fact.fact_id,
        property_id=fact.property_id,
        section=fact.section,
        key=fact.key,
        value=fact.value,
        status=fact.status.value,
        supersedes_fact_id=fact.supersedes_fact_id,
        asserted_at=fact.asserted_at,
        valid_from=fact.valid_interval.start,
        valid_to=fact.valid_interval.end,
        source_id=fact.citation.source_id,
        source_kind=fact.citation.source_kind,
        source_locator=fact.citation.locator,
        source_excerpt=fact.citation.quote,
        source_timestamp=fact.citation.source_timestamp,
        content_hash=fact.citation.payload_hash,
        extractor="ingest.plugin",
        confidence=fact.confidence,
        idempotency_key=f"fact:{envelope_id}:{index:04d}",
    )


def _emit_gap_event(
    store: FactsStore,
    signal: GapSignal,
    *,
    envelope_id: str,
    index: int,
) -> str:
    return store.append_event(
        "gap_signal",
        signal.model_dump(mode="json"),
        idempotency_key=f"gap:{envelope_id}:{index:04d}",
    )


def _emit_observed_event(
    store: FactsStore,
    event: ObservedEvent,
    *,
    envelope_id: str,
    index: int,
) -> str:
    idempotency_key = event.idempotency_key or f"observed:{envelope_id}:{index:04d}"
    return store.append_event(
        event.kind,
        event.payload,
        idempotency_key=idempotency_key,
    )


def _default_fact(envelope: Envelope, *, envelope_id: str) -> Fact:
    return Fact(
        property_id=envelope.property,
        section="ingest",
        key=f"{envelope.kind}-{envelope.payload_hash[:12].lower()}",
        value={
            "envelope_id": envelope_id,
            "source": envelope.source,
            "kind": envelope.kind,
            "content_type": envelope.content_type,
            "payload_uri": envelope.payload_uri,
        },
        citation=SourceCitation(
            source_id=envelope.source,
            source_kind=envelope.kind,
            source_uri=envelope.payload_uri,
            locator=envelope.payload_uri or envelope.payload_hash[:16].lower(),
            payload_hash=envelope.payload_hash,
        ),
        confidence=1.0,
        status=FactStatus.ACTIVE,
        asserted_at=envelope.received_at,
    )

def _now_utc_naive() -> datetime:
    return datetime.now(UTC).replace(tzinfo=None)


def _normalize_datetime(value: datetime | None) -> datetime | None:
    if value is None:
        return None
    if value.tzinfo is None:
        return value
    return value.astimezone(UTC).replace(tzinfo=None)


def _json_dumps(value: object) -> str:
    return json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False, separators=(",", ":"), sort_keys=True)


def _json_loads(value: str) -> object:
    return json.loads(value)


def _row_to_dict(
    columns: tuple[str, ...], row: tuple[object, ...] | None
) -> dict[str, object] | None:
    if row is None:
        return None
    return dict(zip(columns, row, strict=True))


def _row_to_fact(row: tuple[object, ...] | None) -> dict[str, object] | None:
    result = _row_to_dict(_FACT_STORAGE_COLUMNS, row)
    if result is None:
        return None
    result["value"] = _json_loads(str(result.pop("value_json")))
    return result


def _row_to_event(row: tuple[object, ...] | None) -> dict[str, object] | None:
    result = _row_to_dict(_EVENT_STORAGE_COLUMNS, row)
    if result is None:
        return None
    result["payload"] = _json_loads(str(result.pop("payload_json")))
    return result


def _matches_existing_fact(
    existing: Mapping[str, object],
    candidate: Mapping[str, object],
) -> bool:
    ignored = {"asserted_at"}
    return all(
        existing[column] == value for column, value in candidate.items() if column not in ignored
    )


def _matches_current_active_fact(
    existing: Mapping[str, object],
    candidate: Mapping[str, object],
) -> bool:
    return all(
        existing[column] == value
        for column, value in candidate.items()
        if column not in _FACT_ASSERTION_NOOP_IGNORED
    )


def _matches_existing_event(
    existing: Mapping[str, object],
    candidate: Mapping[str, object],
) -> bool:
    ignored = {"occurred_at"}
    return all(
        existing[column] == value for column, value in candidate.items() if column not in ignored
    )


def _build_fact_candidate(
    *,
    resolved_fact_id: str,
    property_id: str,
    section: str,
    key: str,
    value: object,
    status: str,
    supersedes_fact_id: str | None,
    asserted_at: datetime,
    valid_from: datetime | None,
    valid_to: datetime | None,
    source_id: str | None,
    source_kind: str | None,
    source_locator: str | None,
    source_excerpt: str | None,
    source_timestamp: datetime | None,
    content_hash: str | None,
    extractor: str | None,
    confidence: float,
    review_hint: str | None,
    idempotency_key: str | None,
) -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "fact_id": resolved_fact_id,
        "property_id": property_id,
        "section": section,
        "key": key,
        "value_json": _json_dumps(value),
        "status": status,
        "supersedes_fact_id": supersedes_fact_id,
        "asserted_at": asserted_at,
        "valid_from": _normalize_datetime(valid_from),
        "valid_to": _normalize_datetime(valid_to),
        "source_id": source_id,
        "source_kind": source_kind,
        "source_locator": source_locator,
        "source_excerpt": source_excerpt,
        "source_timestamp": _normalize_datetime(source_timestamp),
        "content_hash": content_hash,
        "extractor": extractor,
        "confidence": float(confidence),
        "review_hint": review_hint,
        "idempotency_key": idempotency_key,
    }


def _fact_event_payload(fact: Mapping[str, object]) -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "fact_id": fact["fact_id"],
        "property_id": fact["property_id"],
        "section": fact["section"],
        "key": fact["key"],
        "status": fact["status"],
    }


def _fact_key(fact: Mapping[str, object]) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
    return (str(fact["property_id"]), str(fact["section"]), str(fact["key"]))


def _event_idempotency_key(kind: str, idempotency_key: str | None) -> str | None:
    if idempotency_key is None:
        return None
    return f"{kind}:{idempotency_key}"


def _event_ref(
    event: Mapping[str, object],
    column: str,
    payload_key: str,
) -> str | None:
    value = event.get(column)
    payload = event.get("payload")
    if value is None and isinstance(payload, Mapping):
        value = payload.get(payload_key)
    if value is None:
        return None
    return str(value)


def _event_replay_key(event: Mapping[str, object]) -> tuple[datetime, int, str]:
    occurred_at = event["occurred_at"]
    if not isinstance(occurred_at, datetime):
        msg = "event occurred_at must be a datetime"
        raise TypeError(msg)
    kind = str(event["kind"])
    return (occurred_at, _EVENT_REPLAY_PRIORITY.get(kind, 99), str(event["event_id"]))


def _ensure_transaction_time_axis(time_axis: str) -> None:
    if time_axis in {"transaction", "transaction_time", "tx"}:
        return
    if time_axis in {"valid", "valid_time"}:
        msg = "valid-time as-of queries are not supported in v1; use transaction time"
        raise NotImplementedError(msg)
    raise ValueError(f"unsupported as-of time axis: {time_axis}")


class EventLog:
    def __init__(self, conn: duckdb.DuckDBPyConnection) -> None:
        self._conn = conn

    def emit(
        self,
        kind: str,
        payload: object,
        *,
        event_id: str | None = None,
        occurred_at: datetime | None = None,
        actor: str | None = None,
        fact_id: str | None = None,
        old_fact_id: str | None = None,
        reason: str | None = None,
        idempotency_key: str | None = None,
    ) -> str:
        resolved_event_id = event_id or f"evt_{uuid4().hex}"
        payload_json = _json_dumps(payload)

        if idempotency_key is not None:
            existing = self.fetch_event_by_idempotency_key(idempotency_key)
            if existing is not None:
                return str(existing["event_id"])

        candidate: dict[str, object] = {
            "event_id": resolved_event_id,
            "occurred_at": _normalize_datetime(occurred_at) or _now_utc_naive(),
            "kind": kind,
            "payload_json": payload_json,
            "actor": actor,
            "fact_id": fact_id,
            "old_fact_id": old_fact_id,
            "reason": reason,
            "idempotency_key": idempotency_key,
        }

        existing = self.fetch_event(resolved_event_id, include_storage=True)
        if existing is not None:
            if not _matches_existing_event(existing, candidate):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"event_id already exists with different immutable data: {resolved_event_id}"
                )
            return resolved_event_id

        self._conn.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO events (
                event_id,
                occurred_at,
                kind,
                payload_json,
                actor,
                fact_id,
                old_fact_id,
                reason,
                idempotency_key
            )
            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            """,
            [candidate[column] for column in _EVENT_STORAGE_COLUMNS],
        )
        return resolved_event_id

    def fetch_event(
        self,
        event_id: str,
        *,
        include_storage: bool = False,
    ) -> dict[str, object] | None:
        row = self._conn.execute(
            f"{_EVENT_SELECT_SQL} WHERE event_id = ?",
            [event_id],
        ).fetchone()
        if include_storage:
            return _row_to_dict(_EVENT_STORAGE_COLUMNS, row)
        return _row_to_event(row)

    def fetch_event_by_idempotency_key(self, idempotency_key: str) -> dict[str, object] | None:
        row = self._conn.execute(
            f"{_EVENT_SELECT_SQL} WHERE idempotency_key = ?",
            [idempotency_key],
        ).fetchone()
        return _row_to_event(row)

    def list_events(self) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
        rows = self._conn.execute(f"{_EVENT_SELECT_SQL} ORDER BY occurred_at, event_id").fetchall()
        return [event for event in (_row_to_event(row) for row in rows) if event is not None]


class FactsStore:
    def __init__(self, conn: duckdb.DuckDBPyConnection) -> None:
        self._conn = conn
        self.event_log = EventLog(conn)

    def init_schema(self) -> None:
        for stmt in _SCHEMA_SQL:
            self._conn.execute(stmt)
        for stmt in _INDEX_SQL:
            self._conn.execute(stmt)

    def append_fact(
        self,
        *,
        property_id: str,
        section: str,
        key: str,
        value: object,
        fact_id: str | None = None,
        status: str = "active",
        supersedes_fact_id: str | None = None,
        asserted_at: datetime | None = None,
        valid_from: datetime | None = None,
        valid_to: datetime | None = None,
        source_id: str | None = None,
        source_kind: str | None = None,
        source_locator: str | None = None,
        source_excerpt: str | None = None,
        source_timestamp: datetime | None = None,
        content_hash: str | None = None,
        extractor: str | None = None,
        confidence: float = 1.0,
        review_hint: str | None = None,
        idempotency_key: str | None = None,
    ) -> str:
        resolved_fact_id = fact_id or f"fact_{uuid4().hex}"
        resolved_asserted_at = _normalize_datetime(asserted_at) or _now_utc_naive()

        return self._append_fact_candidate(
            _build_fact_candidate(
                resolved_fact_id=resolved_fact_id,
                property_id=property_id,
                section=section,
                key=key,
                value=value,
                status=status,
                supersedes_fact_id=supersedes_fact_id,
                asserted_at=resolved_asserted_at,
                valid_from=valid_from,
                valid_to=valid_to,
                source_id=source_id,
                source_kind=source_kind,
                source_locator=source_locator,
                source_excerpt=source_excerpt,
                source_timestamp=source_timestamp,
                content_hash=content_hash,
                extractor=extractor,
                confidence=confidence,
                review_hint=review_hint,
                idempotency_key=idempotency_key,
            )
        )

    def _append_fact_candidate(self, candidate: Mapping[str, object]) -> str:
        status = str(candidate["status"])
        if status not in _FACT_STATUS:
            raise ValueError(f"unsupported fact status: {status}")

        idempotency_key = candidate["idempotency_key"]
        if idempotency_key is not None:
            existing = self.fetch_fact_by_idempotency_key(str(idempotency_key))
            if existing is not None:
                return str(existing["fact_id"])

        resolved_fact_id = str(candidate["fact_id"])

        existing = self.fetch_fact(resolved_fact_id, include_storage=True)
        if existing is not None:
            if not _matches_existing_fact(existing, candidate):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"fact_id already exists with different immutable data: {resolved_fact_id}"
                )
            return resolved_fact_id

        self._conn.execute("BEGIN TRANSACTION")
        try:
            self._conn.execute(
                """
                INSERT INTO facts (
                    fact_id,
                    property_id,
                    section,
                    key,
                    value_json,
                    status,
                    supersedes_fact_id,
                    asserted_at,
                    valid_from,
                    valid_to,
                    source_id,
                    source_kind,
                    source_locator,
                    source_excerpt,
                    source_timestamp,
                    content_hash,
                    extractor,
                    confidence,
                    review_hint,
                    idempotency_key
                )
                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
                """,
                [candidate[column] for column in _FACT_STORAGE_COLUMNS],
            )
            assertion_event_id = self.event_log.emit(
                "fact.asserted",
                _fact_event_payload(candidate),
                occurred_at=candidate["asserted_at"],
                fact_id=resolved_fact_id,
                idempotency_key=_event_idempotency_key(
                    "fact.asserted",
                    str(idempotency_key) if idempotency_key is not None else None,
                ),
            )
            if status == "active":
                self._conn.execute(
                    """
                    INSERT INTO active_fact_keys (
                        property_id,
                        section,
                        key,
                        fact_id,
                        activated_event_id
                    )
                    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
                    """,
                    [
                        candidate["property_id"],
                        candidate["section"],
                        candidate["key"],
                        resolved_fact_id,
                        assertion_event_id,
                    ],
                )
            self._conn.execute("COMMIT")
        except Exception:
            self._conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
            raise

        return resolved_fact_id

    def upsert_fact(
        self,
        *,
        property_id: str,
        section: str,
        key: str,
        value: object,
        fact_id: str | None = None,
        status: str = "active",
        supersedes_fact_id: str | None = None,
        asserted_at: datetime | None = None,
        valid_from: datetime | None = None,
        valid_to: datetime | None = None,
        source_id: str | None = None,
        source_kind: str | None = None,
        source_locator: str | None = None,
        source_excerpt: str | None = None,
        source_timestamp: datetime | None = None,
        content_hash: str | None = None,
        extractor: str | None = None,
        confidence: float = 1.0,
        review_hint: str | None = None,
        idempotency_key: str | None = None,
    ) -> str:
        if status not in _FACT_STATUS:
            raise ValueError(f"unsupported fact status: {status}")

        if idempotency_key is not None:
            existing = self.fetch_fact_by_idempotency_key(idempotency_key)
            if existing is not None:
                return str(existing["fact_id"])

        resolved_fact_id = fact_id or f"fact_{uuid4().hex}"
        resolved_asserted_at = _normalize_datetime(asserted_at) or _now_utc_naive()
        candidate = _build_fact_candidate(
            resolved_fact_id=resolved_fact_id,
            property_id=property_id,
            section=section,
            key=key,
            value=value,
            status=status,
            supersedes_fact_id=supersedes_fact_id,
            asserted_at=resolved_asserted_at,
            valid_from=valid_from,
            valid_to=valid_to,
            source_id=source_id,
            source_kind=source_kind,
            source_locator=source_locator,
            source_excerpt=source_excerpt,
            source_timestamp=source_timestamp,
            content_hash=content_hash,
            extractor=extractor,
            confidence=confidence,
            review_hint=review_hint,
            idempotency_key=idempotency_key,
        )

        active_key = self.fetch_active_key(property_id, section, key)
        if status == "active" and active_key is not None:
            old_fact_id = str(active_key["fact_id"])
            existing_active = self.fetch_fact(old_fact_id, include_storage=True)
            if existing_active is not None and _matches_current_active_fact(
                existing_active, candidate
            ):
                return old_fact_id
            if supersedes_fact_id is not None and supersedes_fact_id != old_fact_id:
                msg = "supersedes_fact_id must reference the current active fact"
                raise ValueError(msg)
            return self._supersede_with_candidate(old_fact_id, candidate)

        return self._append_fact_candidate(candidate)

    def _supersede_with_candidate(
        self,
        old_fact_id: str,
        candidate: Mapping[str, object],
    ) -> str:
        if candidate["status"] != "active":
            msg = "supersede requires an active replacement fact"
            raise ValueError(msg)

        old_fact = self.fetch_fact(old_fact_id, include_storage=True)
        if old_fact is None:
            raise ValueError(f"old fact does not exist: {old_fact_id}")

        if _fact_key(old_fact) != _fact_key(candidate):
            msg = "replacement fact must use the same property_id, section, and key"
            raise ValueError(msg)

        active_key = self.fetch_active_key(
            str(candidate["property_id"]),
            str(candidate["section"]),
            str(candidate["key"]),
        )
        if active_key is None or active_key["fact_id"] != old_fact_id:
            msg = "old fact is not the current active fact"
            raise ValueError(msg)

        resolved_fact_id = str(candidate["fact_id"])
        candidate_with_link = dict(candidate)
        candidate_with_link["supersedes_fact_id"] = old_fact_id
        idempotency_key = candidate_with_link["idempotency_key"]

        self._conn.execute("BEGIN TRANSACTION")
        try:
            self._conn.execute(
                """
                INSERT INTO facts (
                    fact_id,
                    property_id,
                    section,
                    key,
                    value_json,
                    status,
                    supersedes_fact_id,
                    asserted_at,
                    valid_from,
                    valid_to,
                    source_id,
                    source_kind,
                    source_locator,
                    source_excerpt,
                    source_timestamp,
                    content_hash,
                    extractor,
                    confidence,
                    review_hint,
                    idempotency_key
                )
                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
                """,
                [candidate_with_link[column] for column in _FACT_STORAGE_COLUMNS],
            )
            self.event_log.emit(
                "fact.asserted",
                _fact_event_payload(candidate_with_link),
                occurred_at=candidate_with_link["asserted_at"],
                fact_id=resolved_fact_id,
                idempotency_key=_event_idempotency_key(
                    "fact.asserted",
                    str(idempotency_key) if idempotency_key is not None else None,
                ),
            )
            supersede_event_id = self.event_log.emit(
                "fact.superseded",
                {
                    "old_fact_id": old_fact_id,
                    "new_fact_id": resolved_fact_id,
                    "property_id": candidate_with_link["property_id"],
                    "section": candidate_with_link["section"],
                    "key": candidate_with_link["key"],
                },
                occurred_at=candidate_with_link["asserted_at"],
                fact_id=resolved_fact_id,
                old_fact_id=old_fact_id,

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated

from anyio.to_thread import run_sync
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from app.core.config import Settings, get_settings
from app.extract.models import ExtractionResult, Fact, FactStatus, GapSignal, SourceCitation
from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope
from app.plugin import ObservedEvent, VerticalPlugin, get_active_plugin
from app.render import collect_render_records, write_building_markdown
from app.schemas.ingest import IngestRequest, IngestResponse
from app.storage.facts import FactsStore, open_facts

router = APIRouter()


@router.post("", response_model=IngestResponse)
async def ingest_envelope(
    request: IngestRequest,
    settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)],
) -> IngestResponse:
    plugin = get_active_plugin()
    envelope_id = _envelope_id(request.envelope)
    extraction = await _extract(request, plugin, envelope_id)
    facts = list(extraction.facts)
    gap_signals = list(extraction.gap_signals)
    observations = await _collect_observations(plugin, facts)
    property_id = facts[0].property_id if facts else request.envelope.property

    def commit() -> tuple[str, list[str], list[str], list[str], list[dict[str, object]]]:
        store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
        fact_ids = [
            _upsert_fact(store, fact, envelope_id=envelope_id, index=index)
            for index, fact in enumerate(facts)
        ]
        gap_event_ids = [
            _emit_gap_event(store, signal, envelope_id=envelope_id, index=index)
            for index, signal in enumerate(gap_signals)
        ]
        observation_event_ids = [
            _emit_observed_event(store, observed, envelope_id=envelope_id, index=index)
            for index, observed in enumerate(observations)
        ]
        envelope_event_id = store.append_event(
            "envelope.ingested",
            request.envelope.model_dump(mode="json"),
            fact_id=fact_ids[0] if fact_ids else None,
            idempotency_key=f"event:{envelope_id}",
        )
        records = collect_render_records(store, property_id)
        return envelope_event_id, fact_ids, gap_event_ids, observation_event_ids, records

    (
        envelope_event_id,
        fact_ids,
        gap_event_ids,
        observation_event_ids,
        records,
    ) = await run_sync(commit)
    await write_building_markdown(settings.output_dir, property_id, records)
    return IngestResponse(
        envelope_id=envelope_id,
        event_id=envelope_event_id,
        fact_ids=fact_ids,
        gap_event_ids=gap_event_ids,
        observation_event_ids=observation_event_ids,
    )


async def _collect_observations(
    plugin: VerticalPlugin,
    facts: list[Fact],
) -> list[ObservedEvent]:
    if not plugin.observers or not facts:
        return []
    observed: list[ObservedEvent] = []
    for observer in plugin.observers:
        for fact in facts:
            for event in await observer.observe("fact.extracted", fact):
                observed.append(event)
    return observed


async def _extract(
    request: IngestRequest,
    plugin: VerticalPlugin,
    envelope_id: str,
) -> ExtractionResult:
    if request.fact is not None:
        return ExtractionResult(facts=[request.fact])

    if request.payload is not None:
        extractor = plugin.extractors.get(request.envelope.kind)
        if extractor is not None:
            return await extractor.extract(request.envelope, request.payload)

    return ExtractionResult(facts=[_default_fact(request.envelope, envelope_id=envelope_id)])


def _envelope_id(envelope: Envelope) -> str:
    return f"env_{envelope.payload_hash[:32].lower()}"


def _upsert_fact(
    store: FactsStore,
    fact: Fact,
    *,
    envelope_id: str,
    index: int,
) -> str:
    return store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id=fact.fact_id,
        property_id=fact.property_id,
        section=fact.section,
        key=fact.key,
        value=fact.value,
        status=fact.status.value,
        supersedes_fact_id=fact.supersedes_fact_id,
        asserted_at=fact.asserted_at,
        valid_from=fact.valid_interval.start,
        valid_to=fact.valid_interval.end,
        source_id=fact.citation.source_id,
        source_kind=fact.citation.source_kind,
        source_locator=fact.citation.locator,
        source_excerpt=fact.citation.quote,
        source_timestamp=fact.citation.source_timestamp,
        content_hash=fact.citation.payload_hash,
        extractor="ingest.plugin",
        confidence=fact.confidence,
        idempotency_key=f"fact:{envelope_id}:{index:04d}",
    )


def _emit_gap_event(
    store: FactsStore,
    signal: GapSignal,
    *,
    envelope_id: str,
    index: int,
) -> str:
    return store.append_event(
        "gap_signal",
        signal.model_dump(mode="json"),
        idempotency_key=f"gap:{envelope_id}:{index:04d}",
    )


def _emit_observed_event(
    store: FactsStore,
    event: ObservedEvent,
    *,
    envelope_id: str,
    index: int,
) -> str:
    idempotency_key = event.idempotency_key or f"observed:{envelope_id}:{index:04d}"
    return store.append_event(
        event.kind,
        event.payload,
        idempotency_key=idempotency_key,
    )


def _default_fact(envelope: Envelope, *, envelope_id: str) -> Fact:
    return Fact(
        property_id=envelope.property,
        section="ingest",
        key=f"{envelope.kind}-{envelope.payload_hash[:12].lower()}",
        value={
            "envelope_id": envelope_id,
            "source": envelope.source,
            "kind": envelope.kind,
            "content_type": envelope.content_type,
            "payload_uri": envelope.payload_uri,
        },
        citation=SourceCitation(
            source_id=envelope.source,
            source_kind=envelope.kind,
            source_uri=envelope.payload_uri,
            locator=envelope.payload_uri or envelope.payload_hash[:16].lower(),
            payload_hash=envelope.payload_hash,
        ),
        confidence=1.0,
        status=FactStatus.ACTIVE,
        asserted_at=envelope.received_at,
    )
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from app.core.config import Settings, get_settings
from app.schemas.health import HealthStatus

router = APIRouter()


@router.get("/health", response_model=HealthStatus)
async def health(settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)]) -> HealthStatus:
    return HealthStatus(status="ok", env=settings.env, version=settings.version)
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from collections.abc import Mapping
from datetime import UTC, datetime
from pathlib import Path
from uuid import uuid4

import duckdb

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated

from anyio.to_thread import run_sync
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends

from app.core.config import Settings, get_settings
from app.extract.models import ExtractionResult, Fact, FactStatus, GapSignal, SourceCitation
from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope
from app.plugin import ObservedEvent, VerticalPlugin, get_active_plugin
from app.render import collect_render_records, write_building_markdown
from app.schemas.ingest import IngestRequest, IngestResponse
from app.storage.facts import FactsStore, open_facts

router = APIRouter()


@router.post("", response_model=IngestResponse)
async def ingest_envelope(
    request: IngestRequest,
    settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)],
) -> IngestResponse:
    plugin = get_active_plugin()
    envelope_id = _envelope_id(request.envelope)
    extraction = await _extract(request, plugin, envelope_id)
    facts = list(extraction.facts)
    gap_signals = list(extraction.gap_signals)
    observations = await _collect_observations(plugin, facts)
    property_id = facts[0].property_id if facts else request.envelope.property

    def commit() -> tuple[str, list[str], list[str], list[str], list[dict[str, object]]]:
        store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
        fact_ids = [
            _upsert_fact(store, fact, envelope_id=envelope_id, index=index)
            for index, fact in enumerate(facts)
        ]
        gap_event_ids = [
            _emit_gap_event(store, signal, envelope_id=envelope_id, index=index)
            for index, signal in enumerate(gap_signals)
        ]
        observation_event_ids = [
            _emit_observed_event(store, observed, envelope_id=envelope_id, index=index)
            for index, observed in enumerate(observations)
        ]
        envelope_event_id = store.append_event(
            "envelope.ingested",
            request.envelope.model_dump(mode="json"),
            fact_id=fact_ids[0] if fact_ids else None,
            idempotency_key=f"event:{envelope_id}",
        )
        records = collect_render_records(store, property_id)
        return envelope_event_id, fact_ids, gap_event_ids, observation_event_ids, records

    (
        envelope_event_id,
        fact_ids,
        gap_event_ids,
        observation_event_ids,
        records,
    ) = await run_sync(commit)
    await write_building_markdown(settings.output_dir, property_id, records)
    return IngestResponse(
        envelope_id=envelope_id,
        event_id=envelope_event_id,
        fact_ids=fact_ids,
        gap_event_ids=gap_event_ids,
        observation_event_ids=observation_event_ids,
    )


async def _collect_observations(
    plugin: VerticalPlugin,
    facts: list[Fact],
) -> list[ObservedEvent]:
    if not plugin.observers or not facts:
        return []
    observed: list[ObservedEvent] = []
    for observer in plugin.observers:
        for fact in facts:
            for event in await observer.observe("fact.extracted", fact):
                observed.append(event)
    return observed


async def _extract(
    request: IngestRequest,
    plugin: VerticalPlugin,
    envelope_id: str,
) -> ExtractionResult:
    if request.fact is not None:
        return ExtractionResult(facts=[request.fact])

    if request.payload is not None:
        extractor = plugin.extractors.get(request.envelope.kind)
        if extractor is not None:
            return await extractor.extract(request.envelope, request.payload)

    return ExtractionResult(facts=[_default_fact(request.envelope, envelope_id=envelope_id)])


def _envelope_id(envelope: Envelope) -> str:
    return f"env_{envelope.payload_hash[:32].lower()}"


def _upsert_fact(
    store: FactsStore,
    fact: Fact,
    *,
    envelope_id: str,
    index: int,
) -> str:
    return store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id=fact.fact_id,
        property_id=fact.property_id,
        section=fact.section,
        key=fact.key,
        value=fact.value,
        status=fact.status.value,
        supersedes_fact_id=fact.supersedes_fact_id,
        asserted_at=fact.asserted_at,
        valid_from=fact.valid_interval.start,
        valid_to=fact.valid_interval.end,
        source_id=fact.citation.source_id,
        source_kind=fact.citation.source_kind,
        source_locator=fact.citation.locator,
        source_excerpt=fact.citation.quote,
        source_timestamp=fact.citation.source_timestamp,
        content_hash=fact.citation.payload_hash,
        extractor="ingest.plugin",
        confidence=fact.confidence,
        idempotency_key=f"fact:{envelope_id}:{index:04d}",
    )


def _emit_gap_event(
    store: FactsStore,
    signal: GapSignal,
    *,
    envelope_id: str,
    index: int,
) -> str:
    return store.append_event(
        "gap_signal",
        signal.model_dump(mode="json"),
        idempotency_key=f"gap:{envelope_id}:{index:04d}",
    )


def _emit_observed_event(
    store: FactsStore,
    event: ObservedEvent,
    *,
    envelope_id: str,
    index: int,
) -> str:
    idempotency_key = event.idempotency_key or f"observed:{envelope_id}:{index:04d}"
    return store.append_event(
        event.kind,
        event.payload,
        idempotency_key=idempotency_key,
    )


def _default_fact(envelope: Envelope, *, envelope_id: str) -> Fact:
    return Fact(
        property_id=envelope.property,
        section="ingest",
        key=f"{envelope.kind}-{envelope.payload_hash[:12].lower()}",
        value={
            "envelope_id": envelope_id,
            "source": envelope.source,
            "kind": envelope.kind,
            "content_type": envelope.content_type,
            "payload_uri": envelope.payload_uri,
        },
        citation=SourceCitation(
            source_id=envelope.source,
            source_kind=envelope.kind,
            source_uri=envelope.payload_uri,
            locator=envelope.payload_uri or envelope.payload_hash[:16].lower(),
            payload_hash=envelope.payload_hash,
        ),
        confidence=1.0,
        status=FactStatus.ACTIVE,
        asserted_at=envelope.received_at,
    )

from __future__ import annotations

from enum import StrEnum
from typing import Annotated, Literal, Protocol, Self, runtime_checkable

from pydantic import (
    AwareDatetime,
    BaseModel,
    ConfigDict,
    Field,
    JsonValue,
    StringConstraints,
    model_validator,
)

from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope, Identifier, PayloadHash, UriString, utc_now

Payload = str | bytes | JsonValue
ShortText = Annotated[str, StringConstraints(strip_whitespace=True, min_length=1, max_length=512)]
Excerpt = Annotated[str, StringConstraints(strip_whitespace=True, min_length=1, max_length=2000)]
GapSignalKind = Literal["missing", "ambiguous", "conflict", "low_confidence", "unsupported"]


class FactStatus(StrEnum):
    PENDING = "pending"
    ACTIVE = "active"
    REJECTED = "rejected"
    SUPERSEDED = "superseded"


class TimeInterval(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    start: AwareDatetime | None = None
    end: AwareDatetime | None = None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def validate_order(self) -> Self:
        if self.start is not None and self.end is not None and self.end < self.start:
            msg = "interval end must be greater than or equal to start"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        return self


class SourceCitation(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    source_id: Identifier
    source_kind: Identifier
    source_uri: UriString | None = None
    locator: ShortText | None = None
    quote: Excerpt | None = None
    source_timestamp: AwareDatetime | None = None
    payload_hash: PayloadHash | None = None


class TransactionMetadata(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    event_id: Identifier | None = None
    actor: Identifier | None = None
    idempotency_key: Identifier | None = None


class Fact(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    fact_id: Identifier | None = None
    property_id: Identifier
    section: Identifier
    key: Identifier
    value: JsonValue
    citation: SourceCitation
    confidence: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    status: FactStatus = FactStatus.PENDING
    valid_interval: TimeInterval = Field(default_factory=TimeInterval)
    asserted_at: AwareDatetime = Field(default_factory=utc_now)
    transaction: TransactionMetadata = Field(default_factory=TransactionMetadata)
    supersedes_fact_id: Identifier | None = None
    extensions: dict[str, JsonValue] = Field(default_factory=dict)


class GapSignal(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    property_id: Identifier
    section: Identifier
    key: Identifier | None = None
    kind: GapSignalKind
    message: ShortText
    source_id: Identifier | None = None
    confidence: float | None = Field(default=None, ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    extensions: dict[str, JsonValue] = Field(default_factory=dict)


class ExtractionResult(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    facts: list[Fact] = Field(default_factory=list)
    gap_signals: list[GapSignal] = Field(default_factory=list)


@runtime_checkable
class Extractor(Protocol):
    async def extract(self, envelope: Envelope, payload: Payload) -> ExtractionResult:
        """Extract facts and gap signals from one normalized payload."""

from enum import StrEnum
from typing import Annotated, Literal, Protocol, Self, runtime_checkable

from pydantic import (
    AwareDatetime,
    BaseModel,
    ConfigDict,
    Field,
    JsonValue,
    StringConstraints,
    model_validator,
)

from app.ingest.envelope import Envelope, Identifier, PayloadHash, UriString, utc_now

Payload = str | bytes | JsonValue
ShortText = Annotated[str, StringConstraints(strip_whitespace=True, min_length=1, max_length=512)]
Excerpt = Annotated[str, StringConstraints(strip_whitespace=True, min_length=1, max_length=2000)]
GapSignalKind = Literal["missing", "ambiguous", "conflict", "low_confidence", "unsupported"]


class FactStatus(StrEnum):
    PENDING = "pending"
    ACTIVE = "active"
    REJECTED = "rejected"
    SUPERSEDED = "superseded"


class TimeInterval(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    start: AwareDatetime | None = None
    end: AwareDatetime | None = None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def validate_order(self) -> Self:
        if self.start is not None and self.end is not None and self.end < self.start:
            msg = "interval end must be greater than or equal to start"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        return self


class SourceCitation(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    source_id: Identifier
    source_kind: Identifier
    source_uri: UriString | None = None
    locator: ShortText | None = None
    quote: Excerpt | None = None
    source_timestamp: AwareDatetime | None = None
    payload_hash: PayloadHash | None = None


class TransactionMetadata(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    event_id: Identifier | None = None
    actor: Identifier | None = None
    idempotency_key: Identifier | None = None


class Fact(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    fact_id: Identifier | None = None
    property_id: Identifier
    section: Identifier
    key: Identifier
    value: JsonValue
    citation: SourceCitation
    confidence: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    status: FactStatus = FactStatus.PENDING
    valid_interval: TimeInterval = Field(default_factory=TimeInterval)
    asserted_at: AwareDatetime = Field(default_factory=utc_now)
    transaction: TransactionMetadata = Field(default_factory=TransactionMetadata)
    supersedes_fact_id: Identifier | None = None
    extensions: dict[str, JsonValue] = Field(default_factory=dict)


class GapSignal(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    property_id: Identifier
    section: Identifier
    key: Identifier | None = None
    kind: GapSignalKind
    message: ShortText
    source_id: Identifier | None = None
    confidence: float | None = Field(default=None, ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    extensions: dict[str, JsonValue] = Field(default_factory=dict)


class ExtractionResult(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    facts: list[Fact] = Field(default_factory=list)
    gap_signals: list[GapSignal] = Field(default_factory=list)


@runtime_checkable
class Extractor(Protocol):
    async def extract(self, envelope: Envelope, payload: Payload) -> ExtractionResult:
        """Extract facts and gap signals from one normalized payload."""

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
refactor: vertical-engine architecture (phases 1-3)

Replace wiki_chunks pipeline with vertical-agnostic engine + bundled
property_de vertical. Engine modules under app/{core,api,mcp,ingest,llm,
extract,skills,storage,render,plugin.py} never import app.verticals.*.

Phase 1: Engine contracts (Envelope, Fact, ExtractionResult, Extractor,
Skill, VerticalPlugin), bitemporal facts.duckdb sink, ingest router with
payload-hash idempotency, async LLM client protocol with StaticLLMClient
fake.

Phase 2: Patcher upsert/supersede with event emission, active/by-section/
as-of queries, render building.md from facts via collect_render_records
that re-stamps activated-pending facts.

Phase 3: property_de vertical with deterministic stammdaten/bank-csv/
camt053 skills, LLM extractors for email/invoice/letter/booking,
KnownSectionsObserver + LegalMapObserver emitting vocab_gap/legal_gap,
hardcoded org allowlist via install_active_plugin registry.

Cleanup: deleted app/services/handlers, wiki_chunks storage, legacy
ask/sim/webhook/wiki/events/lint/properties routes, MCP server/tools/
resources (to be rebuilt in phase 4), 60+ legacy tests. 54 passing tests
remain (engine + verticals).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

# ADR 0001: Bitemporal facts

Status: Accepted

Date: 2026-05-04

## Context

PropContext is moving from generated wiki chunks to a fact store that can be
queried, replayed, and rendered into `building.md`. The store needs auditability:
for any property fact, we need to know what the system currently believes, what
it believed at an earlier point, why that belief changed, and which source
supports it.

The product shape is bitemporal, but the MVP must stay small enough for a
DuckDB-backed 48h implementation. The two time axes are:

- Transaction time: when PropContext recorded or changed its belief.
- Valid time: when the fact is true in the real world.

This ADR defines the v1 behavior before storage code is written.

## Decision

Use immutable fact rows plus an append-only event log.

Facts are assertions. Events are state changes about assertions. A fact row is
never updated to change its value, status, transaction interval, source, or
review state.

### Supersede

Superseding a fact inserts a new fact row and emits a supersede event. The prior
transaction interval is closed logically by the supersede event timestamp and the
new row's link to the prior fact. The old fact row is not updated.

The transaction-time interval for a fact is derived. `tx_from` is the assertion
event timestamp, or the immutable row `asserted_at`. `tx_to` is the earliest
supersede event that names this fact as `old_fact_id`. No supersede event means
the interval is open.

from __future__ import annotations

from functools import lru_cache
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Literal

from pydantic import Field, model_validator
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
        env_prefix="APP_",
        env_file=".env",
        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
        case_sensitive=False,
        extra="ignore",
    )

    app_name: str = "propcontext"
    version: str = "0.1.0"
    env: Literal["dev", "staging", "prod"] = "dev"
    log_level: Literal["DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR"] = "INFO"

    data_dir: Path = Field(default=REPO_ROOT / "data")
    output_dir: Path = Field(default=REPO_ROOT / "output")
    wiki_dir: Path = Field(default=REPO_ROOT / "wiki")
    normalize_dir: Path = Field(default=REPO_ROOT / "normalize")

    anthropic_api_key: str | None = Field(default=None)
    gemini_api_key: str | None = Field(default=None)
    tavily_api_key: str | None = Field(default=None)
    webhook_hmac_secret: str | None = Field(default=None)

    enrich_urls: bool = Field(default=True)
    enrich_max_urls: int = Field(default=5, ge=0, le=20)

    llm_provider: Literal["anthropic", "gemini", "fake"] = "gemini"

    mcp_enabled: bool = Field(default=True)
    mcp_base_url: str = Field(default="http://localhost:8000")
    mcp_required_scopes: list[str] = Field(default_factory=lambda: ["openid", "email"])
    workos_authkit_domain: str | None = Field(default=None)

    fast_model: str = ""
    smart_model: str = ""

    agent_model: str = Field(default="claude-sonnet-4-6")
    agent_max_iters: int = Field(default=20)

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def _resolve_model_defaults(self) -> Settings:
        defaults = {
            "anthropic": ("claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", "claude-sonnet-4-6"),
            "gemini": ("gemini-2.5-flash-lite", "gemini-2.5-pro"),
            "fake": ("fake-fast", "fake-smart"),
        }[self.llm_provider]
        if not self.fast_model:
            self.fast_model = defaults[0]
        if not self.smart_model:
            self.smart_model = defaults[1]
        return self


@lru_cache(maxsize=1)
def get_settings() -> Settings:
    return Settings()

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def _resolve_model_defaults(self) -> Settings:
        defaults = {
            "anthropic": ("claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", "claude-sonnet-4-6"),
            "gemini": ("gemini-2.5-flash-lite", "gemini-2.5-pro"),
            "fake": ("fake-fast", "fake-smart"),
        }[self.llm_provider]
        if not self.fast_model:
            self.fast_model = defaults[0]
        if not self.smart_model:
            self.smart_model = defaults[1]
        return self


@lru_cache(maxsize=1)
def get_settings() -> Settings:
    return Settings()

content.text:
Caveman aside — review-level advice now:

**Constraints to flag before writing code:**

1. **HTTP auth not specified.** Plan says "shared MCP context helpers." For HTTP routes, no design exists. Pick one and confirm with user before building two systems:
   - Simplest: header `X-Org-ID` in dev, FastAPI dependency that calls same `org_can_access` helper. Skip real auth in MVP.
   - Don't re-introduce WorkOS for HTTP — adds complexity outside DoD.

2. **fastmcp 3.2.4 — verify mount API before writing.** Original `mcp/server.py` was deleted. The 3.x line changed how mounts work vs 2.x. Run `uv run python -c "from fastmcp import FastMCP; import inspect; print(inspect.getsource(FastMCP.http_app))"` or fetch context7 docs for `fastmcp` first. Don't pattern-match from memory — they'll need either `app.mount("/mcp", mcp.http_app())` or `app.include_router(...)`-style.

3. **`assert_property_access` currently calls `get_access_token()` from fastmcp.** That only works inside MCP request context. For HTTP routes, you need a parallel helper (or factor a pure `_assert(org_id, property_id)` core that both call). Don't try to make the same function work in both contexts.

4. **`query_demo.py` — use FastMCP in-process `Client`, not HTTP.** Self-contained, no server-spawn. `from fastmcp import Client; async with Client(mcp) as c: ...`. Skips OAuth entirely for the demo.

5. **`add_owner_note` is on the cut-line ("can defer").** Plan §Cut Lines lists it. Tools section lists it as build. Resolve: build it (writes Fact via store with `section="owner_notes"`, status=active, citation source_kind=`owner_note`) — it's tiny and makes cite tool more interesting.

6. **`query_miss` semantics.** Empty MCP query → `store.append_event("query_miss", {...})`. Make sure this doesn't fire on every test that lists empty fixtures — gate it on whether the property exists in allowlist (otherwise tests will pollute event log).

**Approach order I'd take:**
1. Verify fastmcp 3.2.4 mount + Client API (one short script). Don't skip.
2. Build `mcp/access.py` with pure `assert_org_access(org_id, property_id)`. Make context.py and HTTP both call it.
3. Build MCP tools first (closer to DoD). Read APIs after — they're thin wrappers over the same store calls.
4. Tests use FastMCP `Client` for in-process MCP. No HTTP.
5. `query_demo.py` last — proves DoD #8.

Don't start `add_owner_note` until the core read tools work end-to-end. Ship the demo path first.

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import cast

import anyio
from httpx import AsyncClient

from app.render import building_markdown_path
from app.storage.facts import open_facts

PAYLOAD_HASH = "b" * 64
STAMMDATEN_HASH = "e" * 64
STAMMDATEN_PATH = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "data" / "stammdaten" / "stammdaten.json"


def _request_body(payload_hash: str = PAYLOAD_HASH) -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "envelope": {
            "source": "stammdaten",
            "kind": "json",
            "org": "org_demo_berlin",
            "property": "LIE-001",
            "content_type": "application/json",
            "payload_uri": "data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json",
            "payload_hash": payload_hash,
            "received_at": "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z",
        }
    }


async def test_ingest_writes_one_fact_row(client: AsyncClient, settings) -> None:
    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["envelope_id"] == f"env_{PAYLOAD_HASH[:32]}"
    assert len(body["fact_ids"]) == 1
    assert body["gap_event_ids"] == []

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    facts = store.list_facts()
    events = store.list_events()

    assert len(facts) == 1
    assert facts[0]["fact_id"] == body["fact_ids"][0]
    assert facts[0]["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert facts[0]["section"] == "ingest"
    assert facts[0]["source_kind"] == "json"
    assert [event["kind"] for event in events] == ["fact.asserted", "envelope.ingested"]
    envelope_event = next(event for event in events if event["kind"] == "envelope.ingested")
    assert envelope_event["event_id"] == body["event_id"]
    assert envelope_event["fact_id"] == body["fact_ids"][0]


async def test_ingest_duplicate_payload_is_idempotent(client: AsyncClient, settings) -> None:
    first = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())
    second = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=_request_body())

from __future__ import annotations

from app.render.building import (
    building_markdown_path,
    render_building_markdown,
    write_building_markdown,
)
from app.render.from_facts import collect_render_records

__all__ = [
    "building_markdown_path",
    "collect_render_records",
    "render_building_markdown",
    "write_building_markdown",
]
from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Iterable, Mapping

from app.storage.facts import FactsStore


def collect_render_records(
    store: FactsStore,
    property_id: str,
) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
    active = store.list_active_facts(property_id)
    active_fact_ids = {str(fact["fact_id"]) for fact in active}

    records: list[dict[str, object]] = [_with_status(fact, "active") for fact in active]

    pending = (
        fact
        for fact in store.list_facts()
        if str(fact["property_id"]) == property_id
        and fact["status"] == "pending"
        and str(fact["fact_id"]) not in active_fact_ids
    )
    records.extend(_with_status(fact, "pending") for fact in pending)
    return records


def _with_status(fact: Mapping[str, object], status: str) -> dict[str, object]:
    record = dict(fact)
    record["status"] = status
    return record


__all__: Iterable[str] = ("collect_render_records",)
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from collections import defaultdict
from collections.abc import Iterable, Mapping
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import date, datetime
from enum import Enum
from pathlib import Path

import anyio


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Citation:
    source_id: str | None = None
    source_kind: str | None = None
    source_uri: str | None = None
    locator: str | None = None
    quote: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class RenderRecord:
    section: str
    key: str
    status: str
    value: object
    question: str | None
    fact_id: str | None
    citation: Citation
    confidence: float | None


def building_markdown_path(output_dir: Path, property_id: str) -> Path:
    safe_property_id = _safe_property_id(property_id)
    return output_dir / safe_property_id / "building.md"


def render_building_markdown(property_id: str, records: Iterable[object]) -> str:
    safe_property_id = _safe_property_id(property_id)
    render_records = sorted(
        _iter_render_records(safe_property_id, records),
        key=_record_sort_key,
    )

    lines = [f"# Building {safe_property_id}", ""]
    if not render_records:
        lines.append("_No active or pending facts._")
        lines.append("")
        return "\n".join(lines)

    by_section: dict[str, list[RenderRecord]] = defaultdict(list)
    for record in render_records:
        by_section[record.section].append(record)

    for section in sorted(by_section, key=_sort_text):
        lines.extend((f"## {_section_title(section)}", ""))
        section_records = by_section[section]
        active = [record for record in section_records if record.status == "active"]
        pending_questions = [
            record for record in section_records if record.status == "pending" and record.question
        ]
        pending_facts = [
            record
            for record in section_records
            if record.status == "pending" and not record.question
        ]

        if active:
            lines.extend(("### Active Facts", ""))

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from datetime import UTC, datetime
from typing import Annotated, Self

from pydantic import (
    AfterValidator,
    AwareDatetime,
    BaseModel,
    ConfigDict,
    Field,
    JsonValue,
    StringConstraints,
    field_validator,
    model_validator,
)

ASCII_CONTROL_LIMIT = 32
IDENTIFIER_PATTERN = r"^[A-Za-z0-9](?:[A-Za-z0-9_.-]{0,126}[A-Za-z0-9])?$"


def validate_identifier(value: str) -> str:
    if re.fullmatch(IDENTIFIER_PATTERN, value) is None:
        msg = "identifier must contain only conservative id characters"
        raise ValueError(msg)
    if ".." in value:
        msg = "identifier must not contain traversal segments"
        raise ValueError(msg)
    return value


Identifier = Annotated[
    str,
    StringConstraints(
        strip_whitespace=True,
        min_length=1,
        max_length=128,
        pattern=IDENTIFIER_PATTERN,
    ),
    AfterValidator(validate_identifier),
]
ContentType = Annotated[
    str,
    StringConstraints(
        strip_whitespace=True,
        min_length=3,
        max_length=128,
        pattern=r"^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9!#$&^_.+-]*/[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9!#$&^_.+-]*$",
    ),
]
PayloadHash = Annotated[
    str,
    StringConstraints(
        strip_whitespace=True,
        min_length=64,
        max_length=64,
        pattern=r"^[a-fA-F0-9]{64}$",
    ),
]
UriString = Annotated[str, StringConstraints(strip_whitespace=True, min_length=1, max_length=2048)]


def utc_now() -> datetime:
    return datetime.now(UTC)


class ValidTimeHint(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    start: AwareDatetime | None = None
    end: AwareDatetime | None = None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def validate_order(self) -> Self:
        if self.start is not None and self.end is not None and self.end < self.start:
            msg = "valid time end must be greater than or equal to start"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        return self


class Envelope(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)

    source: Identifier
    kind: Identifier
    org: Identifier
    property: Identifier
    content_type: ContentType
    payload_uri: UriString | None = None
    payload_hash: PayloadHash
    received_at: AwareDatetime = Field(default_factory=utc_now)
    valid_time_hint: ValidTimeHint | None = None
    extensions: dict[str, JsonValue] = Field(default_factory=dict)

    @field_validator("payload_uri")
    @classmethod
    def reject_control_chars(cls, value: str | None) -> str | None:
        if value is not None and any(ord(char) < ASCII_CONTROL_LIMIT for char in value):
            msg = "payload_uri must not contain control characters"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        return value

from __future__ import annotations

from app.ingest.envelope import validate_identifier
from app.mcp.orgs import org_can_access


def assert_org_access(org_id: str | None, property_id: str) -> str:
    if not org_id:
        msg = "missing org id"
        raise PermissionError(msg)
    org = validate_identifier(org_id.strip())
    pid = validate_identifier(property_id.strip())
    if not org_can_access(org, pid):
        msg = f"org {org!r} has no access to property {pid!r}"
        raise PermissionError(msg)
    return pid

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

import structlog
from fastapi import FastAPI

from app.api.v1.router import api_router
from app.core.config import get_settings
from app.core.logging import configure_logging
from app.core.middleware import RequestIdMiddleware
from app.mcp.orgs import install_allowlist
from app.plugin import install_active_plugin
from app.verticals.property_de import build_plugin as build_property_de_plugin

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


@asynccontextmanager
async def _lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:
    settings = get_settings()
    configure_logging(settings)
    log.info("startup", env=settings.env, version=app.version)
    yield
    log.info("shutdown")


def create_app() -> FastAPI:
    settings = get_settings()
    plugin = build_property_de_plugin()
    install_active_plugin(plugin)
    install_allowlist(plugin.auth_allowlist)

    app = FastAPI(
        title=settings.app_name,
        version=settings.version,
        lifespan=_lifespan,
        docs_url="/docs" if settings.env != "prod" else None,
        redoc_url=None,
    )
    app.add_middleware(RequestIdMiddleware)
    app.include_router(api_router, prefix="/api/v1")
    return app


app = create_app()
