from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack, asynccontextmanager

import structlog
from fastapi import FastAPI

from app.api.v1.router import api_router
from app.core.config import get_settings
from app.core.logging import configure_logging
from app.core.middleware import RequestIdMiddleware
from app.mcp.orgs import install_allowlist
from app.mcp.server import build_mcp
from app.plugin import install_active_plugin
from app.verticals.property_de import build_plugin as build_property_de_plugin

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


def create_app() -> FastAPI:
    settings = get_settings()
    plugin = build_property_de_plugin()
    install_active_plugin(plugin)
    install_allowlist(plugin.auth_allowlist)

    mcp = build_mcp(settings)
    mcp_asgi = mcp.http_app(path="/")

    @asynccontextmanager
    async def _lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:
        configure_logging(settings)
        log.info("startup", env=settings.env, version=app.version)
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            await stack.enter_async_context(mcp_asgi.lifespan_context(mcp_asgi))
            yield
        log.info("shutdown")

    app = FastAPI(
        title=settings.app_name,
        version=settings.version,
        lifespan=_lifespan,
        docs_url="/docs" if settings.env != "prod" else None,
        redoc_url=None,
    )
    app.add_middleware(RequestIdMiddleware)
    app.include_router(api_router, prefix="/api/v1")
    app.mount("/mcp", mcp_asgi)
    return app


app = create_app()

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack, asynccontextmanager

import structlog
from fastapi import FastAPI

from app.api.v1.router import api_router
from app.core.config import get_settings
from app.core.logging import configure_logging
from app.core.middleware import RequestIdMiddleware
from app.mcp.orgs import install_allowlist
from app.mcp.server import build_mcp
from app.plugin import install_active_plugin
from app.verticals.property_de import build_plugin as build_property_de_plugin

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


def create_app() -> FastAPI:
    settings = get_settings()
    plugin = build_property_de_plugin()
    install_active_plugin(plugin)
    install_allowlist(plugin.auth_allowlist)

    mcp = build_mcp(settings)
    mcp_asgi = mcp.http_app(path="/")

    @asynccontextmanager
    async def _lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:
        configure_logging(settings)
        log.info("startup", env=settings.env, version=app.version)
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            await stack.enter_async_context(mcp_asgi.lifespan_context(mcp_asgi))
            yield
        log.info("shutdown")

    app = FastAPI(
        title=settings.app_name,
        version=settings.version,
        lifespan=_lifespan,
        docs_url="/docs" if settings.env != "prod" else None,
        redoc_url=None,
    )
    app.add_middleware(RequestIdMiddleware)
    app.include_router(api_router, prefix="/api/v1")
    app.mount("/mcp", mcp_asgi)
    return app


app = create_app()

from __future__ import annotations

from httpx import AsyncClient

from app.storage.facts import open_facts

PAYLOAD_HASH = "c" * 64


def _ingest_body(*, kind: str = "json", payload_hash: str = PAYLOAD_HASH) -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "envelope": {
            "source": "manual",
            "kind": kind,
            "org": "org_demo_berlin",
            "property": "LIE-001",
            "content_type": "application/json",
            "payload_uri": "memory://test",
            "payload_hash": payload_hash,
            "received_at": "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z",
        }
    }


def _ingest_with_fact(value: object, *, section: str = "contracts", key: str = "lease") -> dict:
    body = _ingest_body()
    body["fact"] = {
        "property_id": "LIE-001",
        "section": section,
        "key": key,
        "value": value,
        "citation": {
            "source_id": "manual",
            "source_kind": "json",
            "source_uri": "memory://test",
            "locator": "manual",
        },
        "confidence": 0.95,
        "status": "active",
    }
    return body


async def _ingest(client: AsyncClient, body: dict) -> dict:
    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=body)
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
    return response.json()


async def test_active_endpoint_returns_facts(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"counterparty": "Allianz"}))

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/active",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert len(body["facts"]) == 1
    fact = body["facts"][0]
    assert fact["section"] == "contracts"
    assert fact["value"] == {"counterparty": "Allianz"}
    assert fact["citation"]["source_kind"] == "json"


async def test_active_section_filter(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"x": 1}, section="contracts", key="lease"))
    await _ingest(
        client,
        {**_ingest_body(payload_hash="d" * 64), **_ingest_with_fact(
            {"due": "2026-06-01"}, section="obligations", key="annual_meeting"
        )},
    )
    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/active",
        params={"section": "obligations"},
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert len(body["facts"]) == 1
    assert body["facts"][0]["section"] == "obligations"


async def test_obligations_endpoint(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    body = _ingest_with_fact({"due": "2026-06-01"}, section="obligations", key="meeting")
    body["envelope"]["payload_hash"] = "1" * 64
    await _ingest(client, body)

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/obligations",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    facts = response.json()["facts"]
    assert len(facts) == 1
    assert facts[0]["section"] == "obligations"


async def test_pending_endpoint_returns_pending_facts(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    body = _ingest_with_fact({"q": "?"}, section="contracts", key="renewal")
    body["fact"]["status"] = "pending"
    body["fact"]["confidence"] = 0.4
    body["envelope"]["payload_hash"] = "2" * 64
    await _ingest(client, body)

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/pending",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert len(body["questions"]) == 1
    assert body["questions"][0]["section"] == "contracts"


async def test_building_md_endpoint(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"counterparty": "Allianz"}))

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/building.md",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.headers["content-type"].startswith("text/markdown")
    assert "Building LIE-001" in response.text


async def test_cite_returns_citation(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    body = await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"x": 1}))
    fact_id = body["fact_ids"][0]

    response = await client.get(
        f"/api/v1/facts/{fact_id}/cite",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    citation = response.json()["citation"]
    assert citation["source_id"] == "manual"
    assert citation["locator"] == "manual"


async def test_cross_org_denied(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"x": 1}))

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/active",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_hamburg"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 403


async def test_missing_org_header_denied(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    response = await client.get("/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/active")
    assert response.status_code == 401


async def test_empty_active_emits_query_miss(client: AsyncClient, settings) -> None:
    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/active",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json()["facts"] == []

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    misses = [event for event in store.list_events() if event["kind"] == "query_miss"]
    assert len(misses) == 1
    assert misses[0]["payload"]["org_id"] == "org_demo_berlin"
    assert misses[0]["payload"]["property_id"] == "LIE-001"


async def test_as_of_endpoint(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    body = await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"x": 1}))
    assert body["fact_ids"]

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/as_of",
        params={"at": "2030-01-01T00:00:00Z"},
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert len(response.json()["facts"]) == 1

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
from datetime import UTC, datetime

import pytest
from fastmcp import Client

from app.core.config import Settings
from app.extract.models import Fact, FactStatus, SourceCitation
from app.mcp.orgs import install_allowlist
from app.mcp.server import build_mcp
from app.storage.facts import open_facts
from app.verticals.property_de.orgs import ALLOWLIST


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def _allowlist() -> None:
    install_allowlist(ALLOWLIST)


@pytest.fixture
async def mcp_client(settings: Settings) -> AsyncIterator[Client]:
    mcp = build_mcp(settings)
    async with Client(mcp) as client:
        yield client


def _seed_fact(
    settings: Settings,
    *,
    section: str = "contracts",
    key: str = "lease",
    value: object = None,
    status: str = "active",
    source_id: str = "manual",
    source_kind: str = "json",
) -> str:
    if value is None:
        value = {"counterparty": "Allianz"}
    fact = Fact(
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section=section,
        key=key,
        value=value,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        citation=SourceCitation(
            source_id=source_id,
            source_kind=source_kind,
            source_uri="memory://test",
            locator="seed",
        ),
        confidence=0.9,
        status=FactStatus(status),
    )
    settings.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    return store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id=fact.fact_id,
        property_id=fact.property_id,
        section=fact.section,
        key=fact.key,
        value=fact.value,
        status=fact.status.value,
        supersedes_fact_id=None,
        asserted_at=fact.asserted_at,
        valid_from=None,
        valid_to=None,
        source_id=fact.citation.source_id,
        source_kind=fact.citation.source_kind,
        source_locator=fact.citation.locator,
        source_excerpt=None,
        source_timestamp=None,
        content_hash=None,
        extractor="test.seed",
        confidence=fact.confidence,
        idempotency_key=f"seed:{section}:{key}:{status}",
    )


def _structured(result) -> object:
    if result.structured_content is not None:
        return result.structured_content
    if result.content:
        first = result.content[0]
        if hasattr(first, "text"):
            return json.loads(first.text)
    return None


async def test_handshake_lists_tools(mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    tools = await mcp_client.list_tools()
    names = {tool.name for tool in tools}
    assert {
        "get_active_facts",
        "get_pending_questions",
        "get_obligations",
        "time_travel",
        "add_owner_note",
        "cite",
    } <= names


async def test_get_active_facts_returns_seeded(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)

    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "get_active_facts",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert len(payload["facts"]) == 1
    assert payload["facts"][0]["section"] == "contracts"


async def test_cross_org_denied(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)

    with pytest.raises(Exception) as exc:
        await mcp_client.call_tool(
            "get_active_facts",
            {"org_id": "org_demo_hamburg", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
        )
    assert "no access" in str(exc.value).lower()


async def test_cite_returns_source(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    fact_id = _seed_fact(settings, source_id="gmail", source_kind="email")

    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "cite",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "fact_id": fact_id},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["fact_id"] == fact_id
    assert payload["citation"]["source_id"] == "gmail"
    assert payload["citation"]["source_kind"] == "email"
    assert payload["citation"]["locator"] == "seed"


async def test_obligations_filter(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings, section="contracts", key="lease")
    _seed_fact(settings, section="obligations", key="annual_meeting", value={"due": "2026-06-01"})

    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "get_obligations",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert len(payload["facts"]) == 1
    assert payload["facts"][0]["section"] == "obligations"


async def test_time_travel_returns_snapshot(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)

    future = datetime(2030, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC).isoformat()
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "time_travel",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001", "at": future},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert len(payload["facts"]) == 1


async def test_add_owner_note_creates_active_fact(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "add_owner_note",
        {
            "org_id": "org_demo_berlin",
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "notes",
            "key": "first",
            "note": "Heating broken in unit 3",
        },
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["section"] == "notes"

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    rows = store.list_active_facts("LIE-001")
    assert any(row["key"] == "first" for row in rows)


async def test_pending_questions_emits_query_miss_when_empty(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "get_pending_questions",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["questions"] == []

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    misses = [event for event in store.list_events() if event["kind"] == "query_miss"]
    assert any(m["payload"]["surface"].startswith("mcp:get_pending_questions") for m in misses)


async def test_property_resource_returns_counts(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:

toolUseResult.content:
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
from datetime import UTC, datetime

import pytest
from fastmcp import Client

from app.core.config import Settings
from app.extract.models import Fact, FactStatus, SourceCitation
from app.mcp.orgs import install_allowlist
from app.mcp.server import build_mcp
from app.storage.facts import open_facts
from app.verticals.property_de.orgs import ALLOWLIST


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def _allowlist() -> None:
    install_allowlist(ALLOWLIST)


@pytest.fixture
async def mcp_client(settings: Settings) -> AsyncIterator[Client]:
    mcp = build_mcp(settings)
    async with Client(mcp) as client:
        yield client


def _seed_fact(
    settings: Settings,
    *,
    section: str = "contracts",
    key: str = "lease",
    value: object = None,
    status: str = "active",
    source_id: str = "manual",
    source_kind: str = "json",
) -> str:
    if value is None:
        value = {"counterparty": "Allianz"}
    fact = Fact(
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section=section,
        key=key,
        value=value,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        citation=SourceCitation(
            source_id=source_id,
            source_kind=source_kind,
            source_uri="memory://test",
            locator="seed",
        ),
        confidence=0.9,
        status=FactStatus(status),
    )
    settings.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    return store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id=fact.fact_id,
        property_id=fact.property_id,
        section=fact.section,
        key=fact.key,
        value=fact.value,
        status=fact.status.value,
        supersedes_fact_id=None,
        asserted_at=fact.asserted_at,
        valid_from=None,
        valid_to=None,
        source_id=fact.citation.source_id,
        source_kind=fact.citation.source_kind,
        source_locator=fact.citation.locator,
        source_excerpt=None,
        source_timestamp=None,
        content_hash=None,
        extractor="test.seed",
        confidence=fact.confidence,
        idempotency_key=f"seed:{section}:{key}:{status}",
    )


def _structured(result) -> object:
    if result.structured_content is not None:
        return result.structured_content
    if result.content:
        first = result.content[0]
        if hasattr(first, "text"):
            return json.loads(first.text)
    return None


async def test_handshake_lists_tools(mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    tools = await mcp_client.list_tools()
    names = {tool.name for tool in tools}
    assert {
        "get_active_facts",
        "get_pending_questions",
        "get_obligations",
        "time_travel",
        "add_owner_note",
        "cite",
    } <= names


async def test_get_active_facts_returns_seeded(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)

    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "get_active_facts",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert len(payload["facts"]) == 1
    assert payload["facts"][0]["section"] == "contracts"


async def test_cross_org_denied(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)

    with pytest.raises(Exception) as exc:
        await mcp_client.call_tool(
            "get_active_facts",
            {"org_id": "org_demo_hamburg", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
        )
    assert "no access" in str(exc.value).lower()


async def test_cite_returns_source(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    fact_id = _seed_fact(settings, source_id="gmail", source_kind="email")

    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "cite",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "fact_id": fact_id},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["fact_id"] == fact_id
    assert payload["citation"]["source_id"] == "gmail"
    assert payload["citation"]["source_kind"] == "email"
    assert payload["citation"]["locator"] == "seed"


async def test_obligations_filter(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings, section="contracts", key="lease")
    _seed_fact(settings, section="obligations", key="annual_meeting", value={"due": "2026-06-01"})

    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "get_obligations",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert len(payload["facts"]) == 1
    assert payload["facts"][0]["section"] == "obligations"


async def test_time_travel_returns_snapshot(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)

    future = datetime(2030, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC).isoformat()
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "time_travel",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001", "at": future},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert len(payload["facts"]) == 1


async def test_add_owner_note_creates_active_fact(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "add_owner_note",
        {
            "org_id": "org_demo_berlin",
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "notes",
            "key": "first",
            "note": "Heating broken in unit 3",
        },
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["section"] == "notes"

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    rows = store.list_active_facts("LIE-001")
    assert any(row["key"] == "first" for row in rows)


async def test_pending_questions_emits_query_miss_when_empty(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "get_pending_questions",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["questions"] == []

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    misses = [event for event in store.list_events() if event["kind"] == "query_miss"]
    assert any(m["payload"]["surface"].startswith("mcp:get_pending_questions") for m in misses)


async def test_property_resource_returns_counts(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)
    result = await mcp_client.read_resource("property://LIE-001")
    text = result[0].text
    payload = json.loads(text)
    assert payload["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert payload["active_count"] == 1

from __future__ import annotations

from httpx import AsyncClient

from app.storage.facts import open_facts

PAYLOAD_HASH = "c" * 64


def _ingest_body(*, kind: str = "json", payload_hash: str = PAYLOAD_HASH) -> dict[str, object]:
    return {
        "envelope": {
            "source": "manual",
            "kind": kind,
            "org": "org_demo_berlin",
            "property": "LIE-001",
            "content_type": "application/json",
            "payload_uri": "memory://test",
            "payload_hash": payload_hash,
            "received_at": "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z",
        }
    }


def _ingest_with_fact(value: object, *, section: str = "contracts", key: str = "lease") -> dict:
    body = _ingest_body()
    body["fact"] = {
        "property_id": "LIE-001",
        "section": section,
        "key": key,
        "value": value,
        "citation": {
            "source_id": "manual",
            "source_kind": "json",
            "source_uri": "memory://test",
            "locator": "manual",
        },
        "confidence": 0.95,
        "status": "active",
    }
    return body


async def _ingest(client: AsyncClient, body: dict) -> dict:
    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=body)
    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
    return response.json()


async def test_active_endpoint_returns_facts(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"counterparty": "Allianz"}))

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/active",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert len(body["facts"]) == 1
    fact = body["facts"][0]
    assert fact["section"] == "contracts"
    assert fact["value"] == {"counterparty": "Allianz"}
    assert fact["citation"]["source_kind"] == "json"


async def test_active_section_filter(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"x": 1}, section="contracts", key="lease"))
    await _ingest(
        client,
        {**_ingest_body(payload_hash="d" * 64), **_ingest_with_fact(
            {"due": "2026-06-01"}, section="obligations", key="annual_meeting"
        )},
    )
    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/active",
        params={"section": "obligations"},
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert len(body["facts"]) == 1
    assert body["facts"][0]["section"] == "obligations"


async def test_obligations_endpoint(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    body = _ingest_with_fact({"due": "2026-06-01"}, section="obligations", key="meeting")
    body["envelope"]["payload_hash"] = "1" * 64
    await _ingest(client, body)

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/obligations",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    facts = response.json()["facts"]
    assert len(facts) == 1
    assert facts[0]["section"] == "obligations"


async def test_pending_endpoint_returns_pending_facts(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    body = _ingest_with_fact({"q": "?"}, section="contracts", key="renewal")
    body["fact"]["status"] = "pending"
    body["fact"]["confidence"] = 0.4
    body["envelope"]["payload_hash"] = "2" * 64
    await _ingest(client, body)

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/pending",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert len(body["questions"]) == 1
    assert body["questions"][0]["section"] == "contracts"


async def test_building_md_endpoint(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"counterparty": "Allianz"}))

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/building.md",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.headers["content-type"].startswith("text/markdown")
    assert "Building LIE-001" in response.text


async def test_cite_returns_citation(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    body = await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"x": 1}))
    fact_id = body["fact_ids"][0]

    response = await client.get(
        f"/api/v1/facts/{fact_id}/cite",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    citation = response.json()["citation"]
    assert citation["source_id"] == "manual"
    assert citation["locator"] == "manual"


async def test_cross_org_denied(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"x": 1}))

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/active",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_hamburg"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 403


async def test_missing_org_header_denied(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    response = await client.get("/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/active")
    assert response.status_code == 401


async def test_empty_active_emits_query_miss(client: AsyncClient, settings) -> None:
    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/active",
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json()["facts"] == []

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    misses = [event for event in store.list_events() if event["kind"] == "query_miss"]
    assert len(misses) == 1
    assert misses[0]["payload"]["org_id"] == "org_demo_berlin"
    assert misses[0]["payload"]["property_id"] == "LIE-001"


async def test_as_of_endpoint(client: AsyncClient) -> None:
    body = await _ingest(client, _ingest_with_fact({"x": 1}))
    assert body["fact_ids"]

    response = await client.get(
        "/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/as_of",
        params={"at": "2030-01-01T00:00:00Z"},
        headers={"X-Org-ID": "org_demo_berlin"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert len(response.json()["facts"]) == 1

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
from datetime import UTC, datetime

import pytest
from fastmcp import Client

from app.core.config import Settings
from app.extract.models import Fact, FactStatus, SourceCitation
from app.mcp.orgs import install_allowlist
from app.mcp.server import build_mcp
from app.storage.facts import open_facts
from app.verticals.property_de.orgs import ALLOWLIST


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def _allowlist() -> None:
    install_allowlist(ALLOWLIST)


@pytest.fixture
async def mcp_client(settings: Settings) -> AsyncIterator[Client]:
    mcp = build_mcp(settings)
    async with Client(mcp) as client:
        yield client


def _seed_fact(
    settings: Settings,
    *,
    section: str = "contracts",
    key: str = "lease",
    value: object = None,
    status: str = "active",
    source_id: str = "manual",
    source_kind: str = "json",
) -> str:
    if value is None:
        value = {"counterparty": "Allianz"}
    fact = Fact(
        property_id="LIE-001",
        section=section,
        key=key,
        value=value,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        citation=SourceCitation(
            source_id=source_id,
            source_kind=source_kind,
            source_uri="memory://test",
            locator="seed",
        ),
        confidence=0.9,
        status=FactStatus(status),
    )
    settings.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    return store.upsert_fact(
        fact_id=fact.fact_id,
        property_id=fact.property_id,
        section=fact.section,
        key=fact.key,
        value=fact.value,
        status=fact.status.value,
        supersedes_fact_id=None,
        asserted_at=fact.asserted_at,
        valid_from=None,
        valid_to=None,
        source_id=fact.citation.source_id,
        source_kind=fact.citation.source_kind,
        source_locator=fact.citation.locator,
        source_excerpt=None,
        source_timestamp=None,
        content_hash=None,
        extractor="test.seed",
        confidence=fact.confidence,
        idempotency_key=f"seed:{section}:{key}:{status}",
    )


def _structured(result) -> object:
    if result.structured_content is not None:
        return result.structured_content
    if result.content:
        first = result.content[0]
        if hasattr(first, "text"):
            return json.loads(first.text)
    return None


async def test_handshake_lists_tools(mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    tools = await mcp_client.list_tools()
    names = {tool.name for tool in tools}
    assert {
        "get_active_facts",
        "get_pending_questions",
        "get_obligations",
        "time_travel",
        "add_owner_note",
        "cite",
    } <= names


async def test_get_active_facts_returns_seeded(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)

    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "get_active_facts",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert len(payload["facts"]) == 1
    assert payload["facts"][0]["section"] == "contracts"


async def test_cross_org_denied(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)

    with pytest.raises(Exception) as exc:
        await mcp_client.call_tool(
            "get_active_facts",
            {"org_id": "org_demo_hamburg", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
        )
    assert "no access" in str(exc.value).lower()


async def test_cite_returns_source(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    fact_id = _seed_fact(settings, source_id="gmail", source_kind="email")

    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "cite",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "fact_id": fact_id},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["fact_id"] == fact_id
    assert payload["citation"]["source_id"] == "gmail"
    assert payload["citation"]["source_kind"] == "email"
    assert payload["citation"]["locator"] == "seed"


async def test_obligations_filter(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings, section="contracts", key="lease")
    _seed_fact(settings, section="obligations", key="annual_meeting", value={"due": "2026-06-01"})

    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "get_obligations",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert len(payload["facts"]) == 1
    assert payload["facts"][0]["section"] == "obligations"


async def test_time_travel_returns_snapshot(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)

    future = datetime(2030, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC).isoformat()
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "time_travel",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001", "at": future},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert len(payload["facts"]) == 1


async def test_add_owner_note_creates_active_fact(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "add_owner_note",
        {
            "org_id": "org_demo_berlin",
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "notes",
            "key": "first",
            "note": "Heating broken in unit 3",
        },
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["section"] == "notes"

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    rows = store.list_active_facts("LIE-001")
    assert any(row["key"] == "first" for row in rows)


async def test_pending_questions_emits_query_miss_when_empty(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "get_pending_questions",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["questions"] == []

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    misses = [event for event in store.list_events() if event["kind"] == "query_miss"]
    assert any(m["payload"]["surface"].startswith("mcp:get_pending_questions") for m in misses)


async def test_property_resource_returns_counts(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)
    result = await mcp_client.read_resource("property://LIE-001")
    text = result[0].text
    payload = json.loads(text)
    assert payload["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert payload["active_count"] == 1

async def test_cite_returns_source(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    fact_id = _seed_fact(settings, source_id="gmail", source_kind="email")

    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "cite",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "fact_id": fact_id},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["fact_id"] == fact_id
    assert payload["citation"]["source_id"] == "gmail"
    assert payload["citation"]["source_kind"] == "email"
    assert payload["citation"]["locator"] == "seed"


async def test_obligations_filter(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings, section="contracts", key="lease")
    _seed_fact(settings, section="obligations", key="annual_meeting", value={"due": "2026-06-01"})

    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "get_obligations",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert len(payload["facts"]) == 1
    assert payload["facts"][0]["section"] == "obligations"


async def test_time_travel_returns_snapshot(settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)

    future = datetime(2030, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC).isoformat()
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "time_travel",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001", "at": future},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert len(payload["facts"]) == 1


async def test_add_owner_note_creates_active_fact(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "add_owner_note",
        {
            "org_id": "org_demo_berlin",
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "notes",
            "key": "first",
            "note": "Heating broken in unit 3",
        },
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["section"] == "notes"

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    rows = store.list_active_facts("LIE-001")
    assert any(row["key"] == "first" for row in rows)


async def test_pending_questions_emits_query_miss_when_empty(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:
    result = await mcp_client.call_tool(
        "get_pending_questions",
        {"org_id": "org_demo_berlin", "property_id": "LIE-001"},
    )
    payload = _structured(result)
    assert payload["questions"] == []

    store = open_facts(settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb")
    misses = [event for event in store.list_events() if event["kind"] == "query_miss"]
    assert any(m["payload"]["surface"].startswith("mcp:get_pending_questions") for m in misses)


async def test_property_resource_returns_counts(
    settings: Settings, mcp_client: Client
) -> None:
    _seed_fact(settings)
    result = await mcp_client.read_resource("property://LIE-001")
    text = result[0].text
    payload = json.loads(text)
    assert payload["property_id"] == "LIE-001"
    assert payload["active_count"] == 1

"""Print sourced answers from the active vertical over MCP (in-process).

Run after `scripts/ingest_fixture.py` (Phase 5) so the facts store has data.
This script proves DoD #8: at least 5 sourced answers via MCP.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import json
import sys
from datetime import UTC, datetime
from typing import Any

from fastmcp import Client

from app.core.config import get_settings
from app.mcp.orgs import install_allowlist
from app.mcp.server import build_mcp
from app.plugin import install_active_plugin
from app.verticals.property_de import build_plugin

ORG_ID = "org_demo_berlin"
PROPERTY_ID = "LIE-001"


def _structured(result: Any) -> Any:
    if result.structured_content is not None:
        return result.structured_content
    if result.content:
        first = result.content[0]
        if hasattr(first, "text"):
            return json.loads(first.text)
    return None


def _print_section(title: str, payload: Any) -> None:
    print(f"\n=== {title} ===")
    print(json.dumps(payload, indent=2, default=str))


async def main() -> int:
    settings = get_settings()
    plugin = build_plugin()
    install_active_plugin(plugin)
    install_allowlist(plugin.auth_allowlist)

    mcp = build_mcp(settings)
    async with Client(mcp) as client:
        tools = await client.list_tools()
        print(f"# MCP server: {len(tools)} tools")

        active = _structured(
            await client.call_tool(
                "get_active_facts",
                {"org_id": ORG_ID, "property_id": PROPERTY_ID},
            )
        )
        _print_section("Q1: get_active_facts", active)

        contracts = _structured(
            await client.call_tool(
                "get_active_facts",
                {"org_id": ORG_ID, "property_id": PROPERTY_ID, "section": "contracts"},
            )
        )
        _print_section("Q2: get_active_facts (section=contracts)", contracts)

        obligations = _structured(
            await client.call_tool(
                "get_obligations",
                {"org_id": ORG_ID, "property_id": PROPERTY_ID},
            )
        )
        _print_section("Q3: get_obligations", obligations)

        pending = _structured(
            await client.call_tool(
                "get_pending_questions",
                {"org_id": ORG_ID, "property_id": PROPERTY_ID},
            )
        )
        _print_section("Q4: get_pending_questions", pending)

        snapshot = _structured(
            await client.call_tool(
                "time_travel",
                {
                    "org_id": ORG_ID,
                    "property_id": PROPERTY_ID,
                    "at": datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
                },
            )
        )
        _print_section("Q5: time_travel (now)", snapshot)

        first_fact_id = None
        if active and active.get("facts"):
            first_fact_id = active["facts"][0]["fact_id"]
        if first_fact_id:
            citation = _structured(
                await client.call_tool(
                    "cite",
                    {"org_id": ORG_ID, "fact_id": first_fact_id},
                )
            )
            _print_section(f"Q6: cite({first_fact_id})", citation)

    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(asyncio.run(main()))

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from collections.abc import Iterable, Mapping, Sequence
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import UTC, datetime
from pathlib import Path

import anyio

from app.storage.facts import FactsStore

HERMES_EVENT_KINDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
    {"gap_signal", "query_miss", "vocab_gap", "legal_gap"}
)

PROPOSAL_KIND_LABELS: Mapping[str, str] = {
    "gap_signal": "Gap signal",
    "query_miss": "Query miss",
    "vocab_gap": "Vocabulary gap",
    "legal_gap": "Legal-map gap",
}


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Proposal:
    kind: str
    property_id: str | None
    section: str | None
    occurrences: int
    sample_messages: tuple[str, ...]
    sample_event_ids: tuple[str, ...]
    detail_counts: Mapping[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)


def proposals_path(output_dir: Path) -> Path:
    return output_dir / "hermes" / "proposals.md"


def aggregate_proposals(
    store: FactsStore,
    *,
    sample_size: int = 3,
) -> list[Proposal]:
    buckets: dict[
        tuple[str, str | None, str | None],
        list[Mapping[str, object]],
    ] = defaultdict(list)

    for event in store.list_events():
        kind = str(event.get("kind"))
        if kind not in HERMES_EVENT_KINDS:
            continue
        payload = event.get("payload")
        payload_map = payload if isinstance(payload, Mapping) else {}
        property_id = _opt_str(payload_map.get("property_id"))
        section = _opt_str(payload_map.get("section"))
        buckets[(kind, property_id, section)].append(event)

    proposals: list[Proposal] = []
    for (kind, property_id, section), events in buckets.items():
        events.sort(key=_event_sort_key)
        messages: list[str] = []
        event_ids: list[str] = []
        detail_counter: Counter[str] = Counter()
        for event in events:
            payload = event.get("payload")
            payload_map = payload if isinstance(payload, Mapping) else {}
            message = _payload_summary(kind, payload_map)
            if message and len(messages) < sample_size:
                messages.append(message)
            event_id = _opt_str(event.get("event_id"))
            if event_id and len(event_ids) < sample_size:
                event_ids.append(event_id)
            detail = _payload_detail_key(kind, payload_map)
            if detail:
                detail_counter[detail] += 1
        proposals.append(
            Proposal(
                kind=kind,
                property_id=property_id,
                section=section,
                occurrences=len(events),
                sample_messages=tuple(messages),
                sample_event_ids=tuple(event_ids),
                detail_counts=dict(detail_counter),
            )
        )
    proposals.sort(key=lambda p: (-p.occurrences, p.kind, p.property_id or "", p.section or ""))
    return proposals


def render_proposals_markdown(
    proposals: Sequence[Proposal],
    *,
    generated_at: datetime | None = None,
) -> str:
    moment = (generated_at or datetime.now(UTC)).astimezone(UTC).isoformat()
    lines = [
        "# Hermes Proposals",
        "",
        f"_Generated at {moment}._",
        "",
        "Proposals are aggregated from gap signals, query misses, vocabulary gaps,",
        "and legal-map gaps. Review each block manually; nothing here auto-applies.",
        "",
    ]
    if not proposals:
        lines.extend(("_No proposals: no recent gap or query-miss events._", ""))
        return "\n".join(lines)

    for index, proposal in enumerate(proposals, start=1):
        label = PROPOSAL_KIND_LABELS.get(proposal.kind, proposal.kind)
        scope = " / ".join(part for part in (proposal.property_id, proposal.section) if part)
        title = f"{label} — {scope}" if scope else label
        lines.extend((f"## {index}. {title}", ""))
        lines.append(f"- **Occurrences**: {proposal.occurrences}")
        if proposal.detail_counts:
            top = sorted(
                proposal.detail_counts.items(), key=lambda item: (-item[1], item[0])
            )[:5]
            details = ", ".join(f"`{detail}`×{count}" for detail, count in top)
            lines.append(f"- **Top details**: {details}")
        if proposal.sample_messages:
            lines.append("- **Examples**:")
            lines.extend(f"  - {message}" for message in proposal.sample_messages)
        if proposal.sample_event_ids:
            ids = ", ".join(f"`{event_id}`" for event_id in proposal.sample_event_ids)
            lines.append(f"- **Sample event ids**: {ids}")
        lines.append("")

    return "\n".join(lines)


async def write_proposals(
    output_dir: Path,
    proposals: Sequence[Proposal],
    *,
    generated_at: datetime | None = None,
) -> Path:
    target = proposals_path(output_dir)
    body = render_proposals_markdown(proposals, generated_at=generated_at)
    parent = anyio.Path(target.parent)
    await parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    await anyio.Path(target).write_text(body, encoding="utf-8")
    return target


def _opt_str(value: object) -> str | None:
    if value is None:
        return None
    text = str(value).strip()
    return text or None


def _event_sort_key(event: Mapping[str, object]) -> tuple[datetime, str]:
    occurred = event.get("occurred_at")
    moment = occurred if isinstance(occurred, datetime) else datetime.min
    event_id = _opt_str(event.get("event_id")) or ""
    return moment, event_id


def _payload_summary(kind: str, payload: Mapping[str, object]) -> str | None:
    if kind in {"gap_signal", "vocab_gap", "legal_gap"}:
        message = _opt_str(payload.get("message"))
        if message:
            return message
        detail = _opt_str(payload.get("detail"))
        if detail:
            return detail
    if kind == "query_miss":
        surface = _opt_str(payload.get("surface")) or "?"
        detail = _opt_str(payload.get("detail")) or ""
        return f"{surface}: {detail}".strip(": ").strip() or None
    return None


def _payload_detail_key(kind: str, payload: Mapping[str, object]) -> str | None:
    if kind == "gap_signal":
        return _opt_str(payload.get("kind"))
    if kind == "query_miss":
        return _opt_str(payload.get("surface"))
    if kind == "vocab_gap":
        return _opt_str(payload.get("section"))
    if kind == "legal_gap":
        field_name = _opt_str(payload.get("field"))
        section = _opt_str(payload.get("section"))
        if section and field_name:
            return f"{section}.{field_name}"
        return field_name or section
    return None


__all__: Iterable[str] = (
    "HERMES_EVENT_KINDS",
    "Proposal",
    "aggregate_proposals",
    "proposals_path",
    "render_proposals_markdown",
    "write_proposals",
)

    body = render_proposals_markdown(proposals, generated_at=generated_at)
    parent = anyio.Path(target.parent)
    await parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    await anyio.Path(target).write_text(body, encoding="utf-8")
    return target


def _opt_str(value: object) -> str | None:
    if value is None:
        return None
    text = str(value).strip()
    return text or None


def _event_sort_key(event: Mapping[str, object]) -> tuple[datetime, str]:
    occurred = event.get("occurred_at")
    moment = occurred if isinstance(occurred, datetime) else datetime.min
    event_id = _opt_str(event.get("event_id")) or ""
    return moment, event_id


def _payload_summary(kind: str, payload: Mapping[str, object]) -> str | None:
    if kind in {"gap_signal", "vocab_gap", "legal_gap"}:
        message = _opt_str(payload.get("message"))
        if message:
            return message
        detail = _opt_str(payload.get("detail"))
        if detail:
            return detail
    if kind == "query_miss":
        surface = _opt_str(payload.get("surface")) or "?"
        detail = _opt_str(payload.get("detail")) or ""
        return f"{surface}: {detail}".strip(": ").strip() or None
    return None


def _payload_detail_key(kind: str, payload: Mapping[str, object]) -> str | None:
    if kind == "gap_signal":
        return _opt_str(payload.get("kind"))
    if kind == "query_miss":
        return _opt_str(payload.get("surface"))
    if kind == "vocab_gap":
        return _opt_str(payload.get("section"))
    if kind == "legal_gap":
        field_name = _opt_str(payload.get("field"))
        section = _opt_str(payload.get("section"))
        if section and field_name:
            return f"{section}.{field_name}"
        return field_name or section
    return None


__all__: Iterable[str] = (
    "HERMES_EVENT_KINDS",
    "Proposal",
    "aggregate_proposals",
    "proposals_path",
    "render_proposals_markdown",
    "write_proposals",
)

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

from app.hermes import aggregate_proposals, proposals_path, write_proposals
from app.storage.facts import open_facts


def _seed_events(db_path: Path) -> None:
    store = open_facts(db_path)
    store.append_event(
        "gap_signal",
        {
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "contracts",
            "kind": "missing",
            "message": "policy renewal date not provided",
        },
        idempotency_key="gap:1",
    )
    store.append_event(
        "gap_signal",
        {
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "contracts",
            "kind": "missing",
            "message": "counterparty unknown",
        },
        idempotency_key="gap:2",
    )
    store.append_event(
        "vocab_gap",
        {
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "marketing",
            "key": "campaign-1",
            "reason": "section not in known vocabulary",
        },
        idempotency_key="vocab:1",
    )
    store.append_event(
        "legal_gap",
        {
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "section": "obligations",
            "key": "annual_meeting",
            "missing": ["deadline", "responsible"],
            "reason": "legal-map required fields not present",
        },
        idempotency_key="legal:1",
    )
    store.append_event(
        "query_miss",
        {
            "org_id": "org_demo_berlin",
            "property_id": "LIE-001",
            "surface": "mcp:get_pending_questions",
            "detail": "no pending facts",
        },
        idempotency_key="qm:1",
    )
    store.append_event(
        "envelope.ingested",
        {"noise": True},
        idempotency_key="noise:1",
    )


async def test_aggregate_groups_by_kind_section(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    db_path = tmp_path / "facts.duckdb"
    _seed_events(db_path)

    store = open_facts(db_path)
    proposals = aggregate_proposals(store)

    kinds = {p.kind for p in proposals}
    assert kinds == {"gap_signal", "vocab_gap", "legal_gap", "query_miss"}
    gap = next(p for p in proposals if p.kind == "gap_signal")
    assert gap.occurrences == 2
    assert gap.property_id == "LIE-001"
    assert gap.section == "contracts"
    assert "policy renewal date not provided" in gap.sample_messages

    legal = next(p for p in proposals if p.kind == "legal_gap")
    assert legal.section == "obligations"
    assert any("missing: deadline" in msg for msg in legal.sample_messages)

    query = next(p for p in proposals if p.kind == "query_miss")
    assert query.detail_counts["mcp:get_pending_questions"] == 1


async def test_write_proposals_writes_markdown(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    db_path = tmp_path / "facts.duckdb"
    _seed_events(db_path)

    store = open_facts(db_path)
    proposals = aggregate_proposals(store)
    target = await write_proposals(tmp_path, proposals)

    assert target == proposals_path(tmp_path)
    body = target.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert body.startswith("# Hermes Proposals")
    assert "## 1." in body
    assert "Gap signal" in body
    assert "policy renewal date not provided" in body


async def test_no_events_writes_empty_block(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    db_path = tmp_path / "facts.duckdb"
    open_facts(db_path)
    store = open_facts(db_path)
    proposals = aggregate_proposals(store)
    target = await write_proposals(tmp_path, proposals)

    body = target.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "_No proposals" in body

# Wipe runtime artifacts so a fresh demo can run.

set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
OUTPUT_DIR="${APP_OUTPUT_DIR:-$REPO_ROOT/output}"

if [[ -d "$OUTPUT_DIR" ]]; then
  find "$OUTPUT_DIR" -mindepth 1 -not -name ".gitkeep" -print -delete
fi
mkdir -p "$OUTPUT_DIR"
echo "reset complete: $OUTPUT_DIR"

"""Ingest the bundled property_de fixture through the engine pipeline.

Hits the running ASGI app via httpx ASGITransport so the demo exercises the
real ingest route, observers, fact store, and renderer end-to-end.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import hashlib
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

from httpx import ASGITransport, AsyncClient

from app.core.config import get_settings
from app.main import app

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
STAMMDATEN_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "data" / "stammdaten" / "stammdaten.json"
BANK_CSV_PATH = REPO_ROOT / "data" / "bank" / "kontoauszug_2024_2025.csv"
ORG_ID = "org_demo_berlin"
BANK_ROW_LIMIT = 25


def _payload_hash(blob: str) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(blob.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()


async def _post_envelope(
    client: AsyncClient,
    *,
    source: str,
    kind: str,
    property_id: str,
    content_type: str,
    payload_uri: str,
    payload_hash: str,
    payload: Any,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    body = {
        "envelope": {
            "source": source,
            "kind": kind,
            "org": ORG_ID,
            "property": property_id,
            "content_type": content_type,
            "payload_uri": payload_uri,
            "payload_hash": payload_hash,
            "received_at": "2026-05-04T10:00:00Z",
        },
        "payload": payload,
    }
    response = await client.post("/api/v1/ingest", json=body)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.json()


def _read_stammdaten() -> tuple[dict[str, Any], str, str]:
    raw = STAMMDATEN_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    data = json.loads(raw)
    property_id = str(data["liegenschaft"]["id"])
    return data, property_id, _payload_hash(raw)


def _read_bank_rows() -> tuple[str, str]:
    text = BANK_CSV_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    lines = text.splitlines()
    head = lines[: 1 + BANK_ROW_LIMIT]
    trimmed = "\n".join(head) + "\n"
    return trimmed, _payload_hash(trimmed)


async def main() -> int:
    settings = get_settings()
    print(f"# output_dir: {settings.output_dir}")

    stammdaten, property_id, stammdaten_hash = _read_stammdaten()
    bank_csv, bank_hash = _read_bank_rows()

    transport = ASGITransport(app=app)
    async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://demo") as client:
        first = await _post_envelope(
            client,
            source="stammdaten",
            kind="stammdaten",
            property_id=property_id,
            content_type="application/json",
            payload_uri="data/stammdaten/stammdaten.json",
            payload_hash=stammdaten_hash,
            payload=stammdaten,
        )
        print(
            f"stammdaten: envelope={first['envelope_id']} "
            f"facts={len(first['fact_ids'])}"
        )

        second = await _post_envelope(
            client,
            source="bank",
            kind="bank-csv",
            property_id=property_id,
            content_type="text/csv",
            payload_uri="data/bank/kontoauszug_2024_2025.csv",
            payload_hash=bank_hash,
            payload=bank_csv,
        )
        print(
            f"bank-csv: envelope={second['envelope_id']} "
            f"facts={len(second['fact_ids'])}"
        )

    building_md = settings.output_dir / property_id / "building.md"
    print(f"building.md: {building_md} (exists={building_md.exists()})")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(asyncio.run(main()))

"""Aggregate gap and query-miss events into output/hermes/proposals.md."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import sys

from app.core.config import get_settings
from app.hermes import aggregate_proposals, write_proposals
from app.storage.facts import open_facts


async def main() -> int:
    settings = get_settings()
    db_path = settings.output_dir / "facts.duckdb"
    if not db_path.exists():
        print(f"no facts.duckdb at {db_path}; run scripts/ingest_fixture.py first")
        return 1

    store = open_facts(db_path)
    proposals = aggregate_proposals(store)
    target = await write_proposals(settings.output_dir, proposals)
    print(f"wrote {target} ({len(proposals)} proposal block(s))")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(asyncio.run(main()))

Updated task #21 status

# PropContext

**Every building gets a memory that heals itself.**

Property management is context work: every email, invoice, bank transaction,
owner question, contractor update, and meeting decision only makes sense when
you know the building behind it. Today that knowledge is scattered across
inboxes, PDFs, ERPs, bank exports, drives, and the memory of the property
manager who has been there for years.

PropContext turns that chaos into one living building memory per property: a clear,
source-backed knowledge file that people can inspect and AI agents can use
immediately.

https://www.loom.com/share/39c4214527ed4dd5a4bfc712ab65483a 
**[▶️ Watch Demo Video](https://www.loom.com/share/39c4214527ed4dd5a4bfc712ab65483a)** - Click to open in Loom

## The Pitch

PropContext is a self-healing context layer for property management AI.

Instead of making an agent crawl every document again and again, PropContext
compresses the important facts into a dense, readable property wiki. Each
building gets an always-current memory of owners, tenants, contractors, open
issues, invoices, decisions, obligations, and source references.

The inspiration is Karpathy's `llm-wiki` idea: a small, maintained markdown
knowledge base can be more useful and efficient than repeatedly searching a
large pile of raw documents. One compact building memory beats expensive RAG
gymnastics, chunk drift, and repeated context reconstruction.

## The Self-Healing Idea

PropContext does not regenerate the whole memory whenever something changes. It
classifies new information, finds the exact place it belongs, patches only that
section, preserves human notes, and keeps provenance back to the original
source.

Hermes is the self-improvement loop behind the engine: it watches where
ingestion misses facts, where schema gaps appear, and where agents need better
context. From that feedback, Hermes can propose updates to extraction rules,
wiki structure, vocabulary, and ingestion behavior so the system gets better
dynamically instead of staying frozen after deployment.

## Why It Matters

AI agents for property management do not fail because they lack reasoning. They
fail because they lack the right building context at the right time.

PropContext gives agents that context in the most efficient shape: one maintained,
auditable memory per property. The human reads it, the agent acts from it, git
remembers every change, and the ingestion engine keeps learning how to maintain
it better.

input.new_string:
PropContext gives agents that context in the most efficient shape: one maintained,
auditable memory per property. The human reads it, the agent acts from it, git
remembers every change, and the ingestion engine keeps learning how to maintain
it better.

## Quickstart

Requires Python 3.13+ and [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/).

```bash
uv sync                            # install dependencies
scripts/reset.sh                   # wipe output/
uv run python scripts/ingest_fixture.py   # ingest stammdaten + bank fixture
uv run python scripts/query_demo.py        # print sourced answers over MCP
uv run python scripts/hermes_aggregate.py  # write output/hermes/proposals.md
```

Outputs:

- `output/facts.duckdb` — bitemporal fact store
- `output/<property_id>/building.md` — rendered building memory
- `output/hermes/proposals.md` — aggregated gap and query-miss proposals

Serve the API + MCP:

```bash
uv run fastapi dev app/main.py
# HTTP: http://localhost:8000/api/v1/properties/LIE-001/active  (header: X-Org-ID: org_demo_berlin)
# MCP:  http://localhost:8000/mcp
```

## Architecture

- Engine modules: `app/{core,api,mcp,ingest,llm,extract,skills,storage,render}` plus `app/plugin.py`. Engine code never imports `app.verticals.*`.
- Vertical bundle: `app/verticals/property_de/` ships extractors, observers, prompts, schema docs, render template, and an org allowlist. Adding a new vertical is mostly a new context bundle.
- Storage: DuckDB facts + append-only events. See `docs/adr/0001-bitemporal-facts.md`.

## Tests

```bash
uv run pytest
uv run ruff check .
uv run ty check
```

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from collections.abc import Iterable, Mapping, Sequence
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import UTC, datetime
from pathlib import Path

import anyio

from app.storage.facts import FactsStore

HERMES_EVENT_KINDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
    {"gap_signal", "query_miss", "vocab_gap", "legal_gap"}
)

PROPOSAL_KIND_LABELS: Mapping[str, str] = {
    "gap_signal": "Gap signal",
    "query_miss": "Query miss",
    "vocab_gap": "Vocabulary gap",
    "legal_gap": "Legal-map gap",
}


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class Proposal:
    kind: str
    property_id: str | None
    section: str | None
    occurrences: int
    sample_messages: tuple[str, ...]
    sample_event_ids: tuple[str, ...]
    detail_counts: Mapping[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)


def proposals_path(output_dir: Path) -> Path:
    return output_dir / "hermes" / "proposals.md"


def aggregate_proposals(
    store: FactsStore,
    *,
    sample_size: int = 3,
) -> list[Proposal]:
    buckets: dict[
        tuple[str, str | None, str | None],
        list[Mapping[str, object]],
    ] = defaultdict(list)

    for event in store.list_events():
        kind = str(event.get("kind"))
        if kind not in HERMES_EVENT_KINDS:
            continue
        payload = event.get("payload")
        payload_map = payload if isinstance(payload, Mapping) else {}
        property_id = _opt_str(payload_map.get("property_id"))
        section = _opt_str(payload_map.get("section"))
        buckets[(kind, property_id, section)].append(event)

    proposals: list[Proposal] = []
    for (kind, property_id, section), events in buckets.items():
        events.sort(key=_event_sort_key)
        messages: list[str] = []
        event_ids: list[str] = []
        detail_counter: Counter[str] = Counter()
        for event in events:
            payload = event.get("payload")
            payload_map = payload if isinstance(payload, Mapping) else {}
            message = _payload_summary(kind, payload_map)
            if message and len(messages) < sample_size:
                messages.append(message)
            event_id = _opt_str(event.get("event_id"))
            if event_id and len(event_ids) < sample_size:
                event_ids.append(event_id)
            detail = _payload_detail_key(kind, payload_map)
            if detail:
                detail_counter[detail] += 1
        proposals.append(
            Proposal(
                kind=kind,
                property_id=property_id,
                section=section,
                occurrences=len(events),
                sample_messages=tuple(messages),
                sample_event_ids=tuple(event_ids),
                detail_counts=dict(detail_counter),
            )
        )
    proposals.sort(key=lambda p: (-p.occurrences, p.kind, p.property_id or "", p.section or ""))
    return proposals


def render_proposals_markdown(
    proposals: Sequence[Proposal],
    *,
    generated_at: datetime | None = None,
) -> str:
    moment = (generated_at or datetime.now(UTC)).astimezone(UTC).isoformat()
    lines = [
        "# Hermes Proposals",
        "",
        f"_Generated at {moment}._",
        "",
        "Proposals are aggregated from gap signals, query misses, vocabulary gaps,",
        "and legal-map gaps. Review each block manually; nothing here auto-applies.",
        "",
    ]
    if not proposals:
        lines.extend(("_No proposals: no recent gap or query-miss events._", ""))
        return "\n".join(lines)

    for index, proposal in enumerate(proposals, start=1):
        label = PROPOSAL_KIND_LABELS.get(proposal.kind, proposal.kind)
        scope = " / ".join(part for part in (proposal.property_id, proposal.section) if part)
        title = f"{label} — {scope}" if scope else label
        lines.extend((f"## {index}. {title}", ""))
        lines.append(f"- **Occurrences**: {proposal.occurrences}")
        if proposal.detail_counts:
            top = sorted(
                proposal.detail_counts.items(), key=lambda item: (-item[1], item[0])
            )[:5]
            details = ", ".join(f"`{detail}`×{count}" for detail, count in top)
            lines.append(f"- **Top details**: {details}")
        if proposal.sample_messages:
            lines.append("- **Examples**:")
            lines.extend(f"  - {message}" for message in proposal.sample_messages)
        if proposal.sample_event_ids:
            ids = ", ".join(f"`{event_id}`" for event_id in proposal.sample_event_ids)
            lines.append(f"- **Sample event ids**: {ids}")
        lines.append("")

    return "\n".join(lines)


async def write_proposals(
    output_dir: Path,
    proposals: Sequence[Proposal],
    *,
    generated_at: datetime | None = None,
) -> Path:
    target = proposals_path(output_dir)
    body = render_proposals_markdown(proposals, generated_at=generated_at)
    parent = anyio.Path(target.parent)
    await parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    await anyio.Path(target).write_text(body, encoding="utf-8")
    return target


def _opt_str(value: object) -> str | None:
    if value is None:
        return None
    text = str(value).strip()
    return text or None


def _event_sort_key(event: Mapping[str, object]) -> tuple[datetime, str]:
    occurred = event.get("occurred_at")
    moment = occurred if isinstance(occurred, datetime) else datetime.min
    event_id = _opt_str(event.get("event_id")) or ""
    return moment, event_id


def _payload_summary(kind: str, payload: Mapping[str, object]) -> str | None:
    if kind == "gap_signal":
        message = _opt_str(payload.get("message"))
        if message:
            return message
    if kind in {"vocab_gap", "legal_gap"}:
        reason = _opt_str(payload.get("reason"))
        key = _opt_str(payload.get("key"))
        missing = payload.get("missing")
        if isinstance(missing, list) and missing:
            tail = ", ".join(str(item) for item in missing)
            base = f"missing: {tail}"
            return f"{base} (key={key})" if key else base
        if reason:
            return f"{reason} (key={key})" if key else reason
    if kind == "query_miss":
        surface = _opt_str(payload.get("surface")) or "?"
        detail = _opt_str(payload.get("detail")) or ""
        return f"{surface}: {detail}".strip(": ").strip() or None
    return None


def _payload_detail_key(kind: str, payload: Mapping[str, object]) -> str | None:
    if kind == "gap_signal":
        return _opt_str(payload.get("kind"))
    if kind == "query_miss":
        return _opt_str(payload.get("surface"))
    if kind == "vocab_gap":
        return _opt_str(payload.get("section"))
    if kind == "legal_gap":
        section = _opt_str(payload.get("section"))
        missing = payload.get("missing")
        if isinstance(missing, list) and missing:
            first = str(missing[0])
            return f"{section}.{first}" if section else first
        return section
    return None


__all__: Iterable[str] = (
    "HERMES_EVENT_KINDS",
    "Proposal",
    "aggregate_proposals",
    "proposals_path",
    "render_proposals_markdown",
    "write_proposals",
)

lines: -            details = ", ".join(f"`{detail}`×{count}" for detail, count in top)
+            details = ", ".join(f"`{detail}` x{count}" for detail, count in top)

feat(phase-5): hermes proposals + demo scripts

Hermes aggregator (app/hermes/aggregate.py) groups gap_signal,
query_miss, vocab_gap, and legal_gap events by (kind, property_id,
section), counts top detail buckets, and renders one Markdown proposal
block per group. Output goes to output/hermes/proposals.md. No
auto-mutation; review is manual per the locked decision in the plan.

Demo scripts (scripts/):
- reset.sh: wipe output/ except .gitkeep, respects APP_OUTPUT_DIR
- ingest_fixture.py: POSTs stammdaten.json + bank CSV (25 rows) through
  the live ingest route via httpx ASGITransport, writes building.md
- hermes_aggregate.py: reads facts.duckdb, writes proposals.md

Verified end-to-end: reset → ingest (60 stammdaten + 25 bank facts) →
query_demo (six sourced answers, query_miss events on empty surfaces)
→ hermes (one proposal block from the seeded misses).

README quickstart added with the four-script demo loop and HTTP/MCP
serve commands. 76 tests passing, ruff clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
